title: MMIT’23 International Conference - Tashkent International University of Education

1.Modern Education and Pedagogy: Trends, Issues, and Development Perspectives (Current issues in the education system at both the school and university levels, trends and development prospects, as well as new innovative teaching methods and technologies, their comparative analysis with traditional methods and technologies).

title: Bachelor's Degree Applied Mathematics in Tashkent - Training at TIUE

content:
The program “Applied Mathematics” is aimed at those who want to connect their future with the field of education as a teacher in such subjects as mathematics and computer science.

The goal of training in this program is to master theoretical knowledge and acquire practical skills in the field of teaching mathematics and computer science, as well as the development of pedagogical competencies for the training of highly qualified specialists who will be able to carry out pedagogical activities in school education.

The educational, methodological and scientific content of the bachelor’s program “Applied Mathematics” includes various modules aimed at developing knowledge and skills in the field of mathematics and computer science, as well as methods of teaching these subjects.

Forms of training: Daytime education Monday through Saturday.

By ending studies issued diploma Bachelor’s degree by direction “Applied Mathematics” (TIUE).

Structure qualifications consists of from 27-30 mandatory modules, general volume which makes up 480 credits for receipt qualifications.

title: What We Value - Tashkent International University of Education
content:
At Tashkent International University of Education, we believe in the power of education to transform lives. Our institution is built upon a strong foundation of core values that guide us in everything we do. Today, we invite you to discover the values that make TIUE a unique and inspiring place to pursue your education.

We strive for excellence in teaching and learning, encouraging our students to reach their full potential. Our dedicated faculty members provide a supportive and stimulating environment, inspiring students to think critically, engage in meaningful discussions, and develop a thirst for knowledge.

We celebrate diversity in all its forms. Our inclusive community embraces students from different backgrounds, cultures, and perspectives. We believe that diversity enriches the learning experience, fostering mutual respect, and nurturing global citizens who are prepared to thrive in a multicultural world.

We encourage innovation and creativity, empowering our students to think outside the box and find unique solutions to real-world challenges. Through cutting-edge technologies, research opportunities, and interdisciplinary collaborations, we foster a culture of innovation that prepares our graduates for the ever-evolving demands of the future.

We believe in making a positive impact on society. Through community engagement initiatives, volunteer programs, and social responsibility projects, we instill in our students a sense of compassion, empathy, and a commitment to making a difference in the lives of others.

We measure our success by the achievements of our students. With a strong emphasis on practical skills, professional development, and career readiness, we equip our graduates with the tools they need to succeed in their chosen fields and make a meaningful impact on the world.

At Tashkent International University of Education, these core values guide us every step of the way. Join us on this transformative journey, where you’ll be empowered to unleash your potential, embrace diversity, drive innovation, serve others, and achieve your dreams.

title: Admission to Bachelor's Degree in Accounting and Business in Tashkent - Education: in Uzbek, Russian and English
meta_description: Bakalavriat Buxgalteriya hisobi Toshkentda - TIUEda xalqaro diplom olish imkoniyati bilan o'qish. Do you want to enter the Faculty of Accounting and Auditing in 2025? We offer full-time and part-time forms of education. Learn the terms and cost of admission - submit an online application!
content:
TIUE ‘ s Accounting and Business program is aimed at those who want to connect their future with the field of entrepreneurship, finance and accounting. The purpose of training under this program is to master theoretical knowledge and acquire practical skills in the field of accounting and business, as well as competencies to prepare highly qualified specialists who will be able to carry out professional activities in the business sphere. The teaching, methodological and scientific content of the Bachelor’s degree program in Accounting and Business includes various modules aimed at developing knowledge and skills in the field of accounting, financial control and business process management.

Language of instruction: UZBEK / RUSSIAN / ENGLISH

Modes of study: Full-time training Monday through Saturday

A Bachelor’s degree (TIUE) is awarded upon completion of the Accounting and Business degree.

Students who successfully complete the OTHM requirements have the opportunity to obtain the optional OTHM Level 6 Diploma in Accounting and Business (603/4018/6).

Bachelor’s degree requirement: 27-30 compulsory modules (subjects) totalling 480 credits (UK Credit System) must be completed.

Accounting has long ceased to be just a routine profession with calculations based on templates. Modern business requires flexible analysts who understand not only the numbers but also the processes. That is why the specialty “Accounting and Business” remains relevant and in demand worldwide. Having received higher accounting education in Tashkent, you open the way to a prestigious and stable career in the economic and financial spheres.

Our program combines a high level of theoretical knowledge and applied skills, allowing graduates to confidently navigate financial and management tasks. Students study various disciplines of a wide spectrum: from the basics of accounting and audit to business planning and international financial reporting standards. At the same time, the educational process is adapted to modern market requirements and is conducted in Uzbek, Russian, and English.

To apply for an accountant in Tashkent for the full-time program, you need to complete several mandatory steps:

Required documents – ID card (passport or ID card).

To apply for a bachelor’s degree, you must take an entrance exam – either online through a telegram bot or offline on the university campus.

Applicants who successfully pass the entrance exams receive a contract online via the telegram bot.

The online system ensures ease and convenience for applicants during the registration process, guarantees the openness of the admission campaign, and allows applicants to plan their preparation for admission in advance.

After completing these steps, the candidate is officially enrolled in the accounting faculty in Tashkent, and the student begins studying in-person.

The education at our university is aimed at training competent specialists who will feel confident in the conditions of modern economics. We offer relevant content, experienced teachers, and international teaching methods. Our advantages:

Studying at our university is the first step towards a profession that remains relevant and well-paid in any sector of the economy. We invite you to enter the bachelor’s program and become part of the university that shapes the leaders in accounting and finance in the Republic of Uzbekistan.

Join the Accounting and Business Program and open doors to the world of finance and management! Acquire skills that are in demand in today’s society. Become part of the leaders, analysts and creators of successful businesses.

Apply now and start your journey to success in accounting and entrepreneurship!

title: Admissions guideline - Tashkent International University of Education
content:
Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) believes in the transformative power of education. Our university is built upon a solid foundation of core values that guide everything we do. We invite you to discover the principles that make TIUE a unique and inspiring environment for learning, personal growth, and leadership.

Tashkent International University of Education is committed to total transparency and clarity regarding all legal information and institutional documentation. On the following “Legal Documents” page, we provide access to a comprehensive range of policies, regulations, licenses, and permits that govern various aspects of university life.

1.1. Programs taught in UZBEK and RUSSIAN, and the “INTERNATIONAL FOUNDATION PROGRAMME” (IFP) taught in ENGLISH

a) Educational Background: All applicants must be graduates of secondary schools, vocational colleges, or academic lyceums. Candidates are required to submit a standard-form document (a certificate of current enrollment) or a diploma/official transcript.

b) Age Requirement: Applicants must be at least sixteen (16) years of age by December 31, 2026.

a) Undergraduate Requirements: All applicants must be graduates of the IFP program, or a recognised vocational (12-year) secondary school, or another educational institution. Candidates who have completed at least the first year of study at another higher education institution are also eligible. All applicants must provide a standard-form document (certificate of enrollment) or a diploma/official transcript. The University reserves the right to request proof of accreditation for the issuing educational institution if necessary.

b) Graduate Requirements: Graduates applying for Bachelor’s or Master’s programs must provide the relevant diploma confirming their previous higher education degree.

a) Educational Credentials: Original or certified copy of secondary or vocational education certificate (Diploma/Certificate with Transcript).

c) English Proficiency (for IFP only): Official IELTS certificate with a minimum score of 5.0 OR an equivalent recognised qualification (must be valid at the time of application*).

a) Educational Credentials: Proof of 12-year (9+3) education (Diploma/Certificate with Transcript) OR International Foundation Programme (IFP) Certificate OR proof of successful completion of the first year at a local or international university.

If an applicant does not hold an international English language certificate, TIUE provides the opportunity to take an internal English proficiency exam.

3.1. Online Application Only: All enrollment applications must be submitted via the university’s official website (www.tiue.uz). We accept applications exclusively through our online portal.

3.2. On-Campus Support: While the process is online, applicants have the option to visit the university campus to complete their online application with the assistance of our staff.

3.3. Data Accuracy & Documentation: Applicants are responsible for entering their personal data correctly and uploading the required electronic documents to the University Admission System. By submitting the application, the applicant guarantees the authenticity and accuracy of all information provided.

3.4. Confirmation: Upon successful submission of the online application, candidates will receive a confirmation message containing further instructions regarding their application status.

3.5. Admission Timeline: The admission period for TIUE undergraduate programs for the 2026-2027 academic year:

4.1. Entrance Examinations: Upon successful submission of the online application, candidates are required to take the TIUE Entrance Exam, which covers the Language of Instruction and Mathematics.

4.2. Language Proficiency Waiver: Applicants holding a valid international English language certificate (as specified in Section 2) are automatically exempt from the English language entrance exam.

4.3. Exam Format: The entrance examination consists of standardised tests or other assessment tasks designed to evaluate proficiency in the language of instruction and mathematical skills.

4.4. Examination Venue: Exams are held at the following address: Location: Imam Bukhari St., Tuzel-2, Yashnabad district, Tashkent.

4.5. Examination Schedule: Exams are conducted daily, Monday through Friday, in two sessions:

4.6. Fees: The TIUE Entrance Examinations are provided free of charge.

4.7. Results and Enrollment: Exam results will be sent to the applicant within 3 (three) working days. Successful candidates who meet TIUE’s academic requirements will be issued a Tuition Contract by the University Admission Committee.

Dear friends, it is an honour to welcome you to TIUE!

Our team wishes you to move swiftly towards your goals, gain new knowledge and improve your professional experience, striving for greater victories in your life.

title: About Us - Tashkent International University of Education
content:
Established in 2020, Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) is one of the first private international universities, focused on training high-quality professionals in the education sphere.

The Cabinet of Ministers of Uzbekistan adopted a resolution “On the creation of the International University “Tashkent International University of Education” on 23 June 2020, which paved the way for activities of new higher educational institution in Uzbekistan.

Our aim is to create an applied learning environment that enables all participants in the educational process to make discoveries, generate new ideas, critically explore, preserve and pass on knowledge, wisdom and values that will help promote innovation, creativity, research, and entrepreneurship.

Our graduates will become fearless innovators, entrepreneurs, creators and doers. Our faculties, made up of local and international experts, provide a rich and equitable education.

To create an applied learning environment that enables all those involved in the educational process to discover, critically learn, preserve and pass on knowledge, wisdom and values that will help to ensure a dignified existence for present and future generations and improve the quality of life for all.

Tashkent International University of Education will be a dedicated educational center that will fundamentally change the current education system and have a transformative impact on nations through the promotion of innovation, research, creativity and entrepreneurship.

Tashkent International University of Education is committed to creating the best educational environment for our students and the workplace for our staff. Our campus was created in 2021 after a full renovation.

Tashkent International University of Education offers various full-time and part-time educational and foundation programs for prospective students. We are one of the first universities in Uzbekistan, which offers three-year programs.

Strong faculty and academic staff are essential for quality education. Tashkent International University of Education is building a strong team of faculty members, who could play as role-models for our students.

title: General guidance on transfer training - Tashkent International University of Education
content:
1. TIUE offers the following educational programs in Uzbek, Russian and English:

Applications for admission are accepted through the official website of the University ( tiue.uz ) in the form “ONLINE APPLICATION”.

At the same time, applicants have the opportunity to fill out an online application (or questionnaire) at the University.

The applicant must correctly enter his/her personal data and upload the necessary electronic documents to the University’s Document Acceptance System.

At the same time, the applicant guarantees the reliability and accuracy of the information provided.

After uploading the required documents, the employee responsible for document acceptance will proceed to verify all documents submitted by the applicant.

In case of absence of the required document, the employee responsible for document acceptance may contact the applicant.

The University will contact the applicant only at the e-mail address or phone number provided in the online application.

Each applicant undertakes to immediately confirm the e-mail address provided in the online application.

3. Deadlines for accepting documents and taking examinations

TIEU will begin accepting applications from February 1, 2024 through August 31, 2024 for the 2024-2025 academic year.

If necessary, TEEU reserves the right to extend the admission period without prejudice to the preparation for the academic year.

Transfer requirements apply to all TIUE undergraduate programs and are designed to select prospective students with appropriate academic performance and aptitude.

Requirements for transfer to Year 2 of TIUE (for programs taught in Uzbek and Russian) OR Year 1 (taught in English):

Note: In case of discrepancies in the number of credits for subjects (modules) indicated in the academic certificate (transcript), the entrant undertakes in writing to retake the different subjects (modules) at TIU.

Candidates who meet TIUE requirements and hold a recognized International Foundation Program certificate or diploma may be admitted upon presentation of an English language proficiency certificate or successful completion of an internal entrance examination.

TIEU reserves the right to provide additional direct admission (transfer) options that may differ from normal practice.

After the application and all submitted documents have been reviewed, within 7 (seven) working days, a letter with a proposal on the conditions for continuing studies at TIUE is sent to the enrollee’s e-mail address registered on the TIUE Applicant Registration Portal.

The University’s Admissions Committee will present a tuition fee agreement to applicants who have expressed a desire to transfer and agreed to the terms of the transfer within a certain period of time after the submission of the originals of all required documents.

After the applicant has fulfilled his/her financial obligations under the tuition fee agreement, TIUE will issue an order for the applicant’s admission to the University.

The order will be issued in September or closer to the beginning of the academic year.

TIEU has the right to unilaterally terminate the study contract if the applicant has provided false information, forged documents and/or is suspected of cheating in the enrollment and/or examination process. This condition applies even after admission to the University.

title: Enroll in the MSc Accounting and Finance Master's program in Tashkent - Accounting and Finance: Enroll in 2025
meta_description: Master's Degree Accounting and Finance MSc - To obtain a Master's degree in MSc Accounting and Finance. How should I proceed and what documents are needed? The program provides academic knowledge and practical skills for a successful career. Find out the price and apply online!
content:
TIUE ‘s Master of Science in Accounting and Finance program is aimed at those who want to link their future to the field of accounting and finance as a highly qualified professional, higher education educator, and professional academic researcher.

The purpose of training under this program is to master theoretical knowledge and acquire practical skills in the field of accounting and finance, to develop pedagogical competencies for the training of highly qualified specialists who will be able to carry out teaching activities in higher education, as well as to conduct research activities.

The teaching, methodological and scientific content of the Master’s program in Accounting and Finance includes various modules aimed at developing knowledge and skills in accounting and finance, as well as scientific research in this field.

26 compulsory modules totaling 480 credits for the qualification.

| ACCOUNTING AND FINANCE (MSC) | Full-time study | 1,5 | 25 000 000 | 2 000 | 30 000 000 | 2 400 |

A master’s degree in accounting and finance opens up new horizons for graduates in terms of professional development. This programme combines practical accounting skills with a strategic understanding of financial processes. With the growing demand for qualified specialists in Uzbekistan, the programme provides TIUE students with the opportunity to gain knowledge that is in demand in both private companies and government agencies.

The programme is designed for those who seek to deepen their education in accounting and finance and build a career in business or academia. The course is unique in its combination of classical disciplines and modern approaches, enabling graduates to be prepared to solve practical problems. The Master’s programme in Accounting provides both theoretical training and practical work with real-life case studies.

Students on the programme study a wide range of disciplines, including:

This approach gives future specialists a comprehensive understanding of financial processes. An important advantage is that Master’s students in accounting gain skills that can be applied in various areas of business. The university takes international experience into account and combines theoretical knowledge with practical training, which makes the programme particularly attractive to students.

Special attention is paid to language training. The programme is available in Uzbek, Russian and English, which broadens the opportunities for graduates. The full-time format of the classes allows students to immerse themselves more deeply in the learning process and receive feedback from teachers. The faculty has modern educational standards, so TIUE students receive training that meets international requirements.

An important advantage of the programme is its flexibility in choosing a career path. Graduates can work in banks, financial organisations, auditing companies, as well as in enterprises in the real sector of the economy. For those who plan to develop in the academic field, the programme provides research projects and the opportunity for publications.

Tuition fees depend on the admission conditions and may be adjusted from year to year. It is important that the university maintains transparency and provides students with complete information about funding opportunities and scholarship programmes. This approach allows future specialists to plan their expenses in advance and build career prospects.

title: Bachelor's Cybersecurity in Tashkent - Profession of the Future: Enroll at TIUE University

content:
The goal of the Cyber Security program is to form and develop professional competencies in students, allowing them to apply modern information protection technologies, choose means and tools of information protection, minimizing threats of unauthorised access to data.

The development of digital technologies has led to the emergence of a large number of digital threats on the Internet.

In response to the threats, a field of knowledge has emerged that deals with the development and implementation of technologies to protect information systems – cybersecurity.

The goal of cybersecurity is to ensure the three most important principles: confidentiality, integrity and availability.

Today, the issue of cybersecurity of various government agencies, data of commercial organisations, and personal data of individuals, to which access must be restricted, is extremely acute.

The program is aimed at training researchers, analysts and developers in the field of information protection.

The peculiarity of the program is specialisation in the field of development and application of mathematical models and methods for data protection in information systems of various purposes, as well as specialised software.

Upon graduation, a Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity (TIUE) is awarded

The structure of the qualification consists of 27-30 compulsory modules, totalling 480 credits towards the qualification.

The world is changing rapidly, and the importance of reliable protection of digital systems is increasing day by day. In conditions of constant threats – from viruses to targeted attacks on corporate networks – training a cybersecurity specialist opens up excellent prospects, as it is a stable and highly paid profession. By receiving a quality education in this field, you can build a career in one of the most promising IT fields. It is precisely this opportunity that our university offers within the framework of the bachelor’s program in cybersecurity.

Modern challenges require a comprehensive approach to training specialists. Our program covers both technical and management aspects, providing students with fundamental training and practical skills in ensuring security in the digital environment. Education is provided in Uzbek, Russian, and English, and includes both practical and theoretical subjects based on international standards.

Such an approach forms a reliable theoretical base and applied skills in students, allowing them to adapt to rapidly developing technologies and challenges of the digital age.

Applicants can be admitted to the full-time form of study according to the standard procedure. For convenience and transparency, all stages are described on our website.

Completion of all stages opens the way to obtaining the status of a student in a popular speciality, where the educational process is integrated with practical training for professional activity in the modern digital environment.

Cybersecurity specialist is the most promising profession of the future!Every day, organizations are becoming more and more dependent on the data that drives their business, which is why protecting this valuable information is becoming a top priority.

The payment of the contract amount can be divided into 4 parts and paid in 25% installments according to the schedule specified in the contract.

25% of the contract amount – within 15 days of receipt of the contract;

If you are unable to make your contract payment within the specified timeframe, you should contact the University’s Accounting department, explain your situation and agree a possible payment deadline.

If exceptionally necessary, there is an option to pay for the contract on a monthly basis. This requires notifying the university administration and setting up a separate payment schedule in writing and paying the first installment of 25% of the contract amount to begin with.

Based on market relations and market conditions in the field of higher education services, the University reserves the right to make changes in the amount of the tuition contract for one academic year. At the same time, the university has a policy of keeping the contract amount unchanged from freshman year to graduation.

Every year the Ministry of Higher Education, Science and Innovation allocates a certain number of quotas for grant places in all areas. Candidates are elected on the basis of a special examination among willing students. Currently, 35 students at the university are studying on a government grant. Information on grant locations will be updated once the appropriate documents are approved.

The TIUE Education Center was founded to implement additional educational programs to prepare students of schools, academic lyceums and colleges for admission to TIUE and other higher educational institutions of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

We enable students and graduates from high schools, high schools and colleges to explore academic opportunities at TIUE. This allows them to experience university life and adjust well to their studies. Overall, this approach makes a positive change in the personality of the student and all the components of success are deeply imprinted in their memory, preparing them for all academic challenges in the future.

At present, the university does not provide dormitory accommodation to students.

TIUE does not currently award scholarships or financial aid to students.

Beginning in February, every week on (Thursday) from 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m., university staff and student volunteers will host an “Open House” for applicants who want to familiarize themselves with the campus and see firsthand the learning process and the busy lives of our students.

Students are required to wear clothing that meets the University’s requirements.

TIUE International University was established according to the Resolution of the Cabinet of Ministers of the Republic of Uzbekistan “On Establishment of Tashkent International University of Education” dated June 23, 2020, No. 404. The University has License No. 331829 dated July 30, 2024of the authorized state body for the implementation of activities on the provision of higher education services.

The University carries out its activities in accordance with the current legislation of the Republic of Uzbekistan, fully complies with the licensing requirements, observes the quality of education, improves the material and technical base and has a strong and highly qualified faculty.

We are located at: Tashkent city, Yashnabad district, Tuzel 2, Imam Bukhari street,(geoposition, video guide)

TIUE administration does not prevent students from participating in Work and Travel programs with the condition of mandatory return to study. Students independently apply for these programs and fulfill the requirements of the organizers. The University can provide the student with the necessary documentation.

Students are given a two-week winter vacation for New Year’s Eve, a spring vacation for Nowruz holiday, and 3 months of summer and fall vacations (July, August, September). You can read the Academic Calendar in more detail at the following links: ACADEMIC CALENDAR

Classes are taught according to the currently current class schedule. Study takes 4-5 days a week, and the class schedule is tailored to the classrooms and teachers’ schedules.

The University supports all endeavors of our students. In doing so, students should assess and clearly understand their capabilities, how much time they can devote to work without compromising their studies. The University has a policy that LEARNING IS A PRIORITY, so you should allow plenty of time for classes, homework and exam preparation before taking a job.

title: Facts & Figures - Tashkent International University of Education
content:
Tashkent International University of Education was founded in 2020 and it is one of the first private universities of Uzbekistan.

As of 1 June 2023, over 850 students are studying in various educational programs of Tashkent International University of Education. The students have various social-economic and educational backgrounds and are from various parts of Uzbekistan.

title: E-LIBRARY - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: OUR WEB E-LIBRARY
content:
Welcome! The University E-LIBRARY TIUE provides you access to thousands of electronic books. In our library, you can quickly and conveniently find the necessary textbooks, scientific articles, and educational materials.

E-LIBRARY offers thousands of electronic books on various subjects. Use the filtering and search system to find the book you need. Electronic books are available for download and reading at any time.

E-LIBRARY allows you to get acquainted with all the leading authors of the university. This page contains the works of each author and their brief biographies.

Through your personal profile, you can view saved books, track your reading history, and add new books. Manage your electronic library!

title: Master's degree in Data Science in Tashkent - Data Science: Enroll in MSc Master's program
meta_description: To obtain a Master's degree in Data Science in Tashkent - Training in Data Science with an international diploma at TIUE. Do you want to study at the prestigious Master's program in Data Science in 2025? We offer modern programs, instruction in Russian, Uzbek, and English. Check out the admission terms and prices on our website!
content:
TIUE ‘s Master of Science in Data Science program is aimed at those who want to connect their future to the field of data science as a highly skilled professional, higher education educator, and professional scientific researcher.

The teaching, methodological and scientific content of the Master’s program in Data Science includes various modules aimed at developing knowledge and skills in data science as well as scientific research in this field

A Master’s degree (TIUE ) is awarded upon completion of the Data Science specialization.

Students who successfully complete their studies in English and in accordance with OTHM requirements have the opportunity to obtain an additional OTHM Level 7 Diploma in Data Science (610/2153/2).

Master’s Degree Requirement: 6 compulsory modules (subjects) totaling 180 credits (UK Credit System) must be mastered.

Classes are held during the day in the form of lectures, seminars, practical classes, master classes and industrial practice.

Scientific and pedagogical work and thesis are carried out under the guidance of a supervisor.

Head of the Department of Information Technology and Mathematics Tashkent State Technical University named after Abu Rayhon Beruni,

All credits are listed under the UK education system. Depending on the language of instruction, there may be slight differences in the Curriculum. At the same time, the University has the right to make some changes and additions to the Curriculum.

Associate Professor of the Department of Information Technology and Mathematics, PhD in Physics and Mathematics

Doctor of Philosophy (PhD), Candidate of Physical and Mathematical Sciences, Associate Professor of the Department of Information Technology and Mathematics

PhD, Senior Lecturer of the Department of Information Technology and Mathematics

Tashkent University of Information Technologies named after Muhammad al-Khwarizmi, 2018

Lecturer of the Department of Information Technology and Mathematics

Mirzo Ulugbek National University of Uzbekistan, 2019

Today’s world is built on data. Behind every technology, service or application lies a complex system for collecting, analysing and processing information. That is why the profession of scientist is not just a trend, but also a response to the challenges of the digital economy. Applicants choose the Data Science master’s programme because it combines mathematics, computer science and machine intelligence — a set of skills necessary for effective solutions and real impact on the future.

Studying for a master’s degree in Data Science at TIUE is an opportunity to delve deeper into the tools and methods of working with large amounts of information. Our university provides not just a basic education, but a comprehensive view of modern technologies that are used in business, science and government systems. The high level of education and specialised training are aimed at developing specialists who are able to find the optimal solution to complex problems.

Each module of the Data Science programme is aimed at developing the skills necessary for a successful career in the rapidly developing field of data processing. Students learn to build models, search for patterns, make informed decisions and present the results of their work.

We strive to create conditions that allow each student to unlock their potential and confidently move towards their chosen profession. A master’s degree in Data Science is not just an education, but the start of a real career.

These advantages make learning effective and meaningful. We want students to not only study theory, but also develop as professionals capable of making strategic decisions based on data. Here you can enrol in a Master’s programme in Data Science in Tashkent, become part of the university community, master modern methods and obtain a Master’s degree that is in demand in any country.

To apply for a master’s degree in data science, it is important to familiarise yourself with the key requirements in advance. We strive to make the admission process transparent and understandable so that every applicant who decides to apply to our master’s programme in Tashkent feels confident and supported.

If you are ready to enrol in the Data Science master’s programme in Tashkent, we invite you to contact us. Our Data Science faculty welcomes those who strive to develop the field, form new approaches and work with what shapes the future — with such an important criterion as data.

Join the Data Science (MSc) Program and open doors to the world of data and analytics! Acquire skills that are in demand in today’s society. Become part of the experts, analysts and innovators creating a data-driven future.

Apply now and start your path to success in Data Science!

title: Enroll in the Bachelor's Degree in Business Management in Tashkent - TIUE University: Russian, Uzbek and English languages
meta_description: You can apply for a Bachelor's degree in Business Management in Tashkent, either in person or on a part-time basis, in Uzbek or English. What are the conditions and cost of education in 2025? At TIUE, you will receive higher education and international perspectives. Apply now!
content:
The objective of the “Business Management” programme is the graduation of students with theoretical knowledge and practical skills in the field of business management. They will have a fundamental base for a professional career in companies and organisations operating both nationally and internationally. The content of the Bachelor’s degree in “Business Management” includes modules that develop knowledge and skills in accounting, business analytics, communication, economics, entrepreneurship, finance, international business, law, leadership, marketing, operations management, people management, strategy and sustainable business practices. This content is relevant and applicable within the country and on the world stage.

Languages of instruction: English / Uzbek / Russian

Upon graduation, a bachelor’s degree is awarded in BUSINESS MANAGEMENT (TIUE).

Lecturer at the Department of Business Management,

Professor of the Department of Business Management

Associate Professor of “Business Management” Department

Degree of Candidate of Economic Sciences (PhD) – Institute of Economics at the Academy of Sciences of Uzbekistan, 1990

Master of Business Administration (MBA) degree – University of Sunderland (UK), 2018

Payment-amount of contract PROGRAMS IN UZBEK AND RUSSIAN LANGUAGES |

*) International students in Uzbekistan pay the specified tuition fee. Outside of Uzbekistan, payment is made in US dollars at the exchange rate set by the Central Bank of the Republic of Uzbekistan on the day of payment.

The modern labor market requires not only deep theoretical knowledge but also applied management skills. The Business Management Bachelor’s program, developed by highly qualified specialists, is a choice for those seeking professional growth in project management, entrepreneurship, marketing, and corporate governance. For those wishing to enter the Faculty of Business Management in Tashkent, we offer quality education focused on the real demands of the business world.

The educational program covers key aspects and directions of modern management, combining academic base and practical training. Students receive knowledge in the fields of economics, marketing, strategic management, and business analysis. The education is aimed at developing critical thinking, analytical skills, and necessary qualifications for making management decisions.

This business-focused educational strategy enables graduates to seamlessly adapt to various roles related to managing teams, projects, and operational workflows in the corporate world.

To enroll in the full-time form of study, you need to complete several steps. It’s important to familiarize yourself with the requirements in advance to complete all stages of admission without delays and with complete confidence in the result.

Required documents – ID card (passport or ID card).

To apply for a bachelor’s degree, you must take an entrance exam – either online through a telegram bot or offline on the university campus.

Applicants who successfully pass the entrance exams receive a contract online via the

To apply to the master’s program, you need to take an entrance interview and submit documents for the relevant field through a telegram bot.

The online system ensures ease and convenience for applicants during the registration process, guarantees the openness of the admission campaign, and allows applicants to plan their preparation for admission in advance.

Do you want to gain complete business competencies, extend your business practices with leadership skills and start your business project or career in a well-known company while working on your personal development? Then apply for training in the “Business Management” programme.

title: Enroll in the Master's program Theory of Pedagogy in Tashkent - Training at the university TIUE: cost
meta_description: Master's Studies Theory and History of Pedagogy in Tashkent in Russian and Uzbek languages at affordable prices. How should I proceed and how much does the training cost? Submit your documents and get a prestigious pedagogical education. Learn more and apply online!
content:
TIUE ‘s Master’s program “Theory and History of Pedagogy” is aimed at those who want to connect their future with the field of education in a higher education teacher as well as a professional academic researcher.

The purpose of training under this program is to master theoretical knowledge and acquire practical skills in the science of pedagogy, to develop pedagogical competencies for the training of highly qualified specialists who will be able to carry out pedagogical activities in higher education, as well as to conduct research activities.

The teaching, methodological and scientific content of the Master’s program in “Theory and History of Pedagogy” includes various modules aimed at developing knowledge and skills in the field of pedagogical science, as well as research in education.

Modes of study:Full-time training Monday through Saturday.

Upon graduation, a Master’s degree (TIUE) is awarded inTheory and History of Pedagogy.

Head of Department “Education and Training Management”

Professor, Department of “Education and Training Management”

Doctor of Science degree (DSc) – Mirzo Ulugbek National University of Uzbekistan, 2022

teacher of the department “Education and Training Management”

Master’s Degree in Psychology – Mirzo Ulugbek National University of Uzbekistan, 2022

Lecturer, Department of “Education and Training Management”

| THEORY AND HISTORY OF PEDAGOGY (MSC) | Full-time study | 1,5 | 23 000 000 | 1 800 | X | X |

English language proficiency or submission of an English language certificate (IELTS 5.0 or above or equivalent)

Developing education means shaping the future. If you feel the desire to influence the education system, build theoretical foundations and study pedagogy in greater depth, a master’s degree in this field will be a step in the right direction. We have created a programme that combines a scientific foundation with practical application so that you can build a conscious and relevant academic career.

The Master’s programme in Theory and History of Pedagogy (MSC) is designed for those who aspire not only to work in the field of education, but also to understand it in depth. This is a scientific and academic programme that focuses on the historical development of pedagogical concepts and contemporary theoretical approaches. Our approach helps students develop a systematic understanding of the processes of learning, education and development. What sets the programme apart:

This programme is suitable for those who are interested in scientific work, teaching or want to develop educational strategies and methodologies based on a rich historical context and modern pedagogical theories.

We offer not just a diploma, but a solid foundation for professional and academic growth. Our Master’s programme in pedagogy is an opportunity to combine scientific interest with practice and become an expert whose opinion really matters in the educational environment. Why choose us:

Master’s degree studies are not just a continuation of higher education. They are an opportunity to change the educational space, become a participant in scientific research and prepare for a teaching career at a new level.

Join the Theory and History of Pedagogy (MSc) Program and open doors to the world of education and science!

Acquire the knowledge and skills that are in demand in today’s society.

Become part of the educators, researchers and creators of future generations.

Apply now and start your path to success in the field of pedagogy and educational research!

title: День студента в TIUE! - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: День студента в TIUE!
content:
16 ноября в Tashkent International University of Education пройдет День студента в формате КВН. Приглашаем студентов создать команды до 10 человек и проявить свои таланты!

1️⃣ Представление команды – у вас будет 5 минут, чтобы оригинально представить свою команду.

2️⃣ Вопрос-ответ – короткие вопросы по 30 секунд на каждый ответ, где важны скорость и сообразительность.

3️⃣ Миниатюра – еще 5 минут на творческое выступление, которое покажет вашу команду в наилучшем виде.

Лучшие команды получат призы от университета! Присоединяйтесь и участвуйте в студенческой жизни университета!

title: Dear graduates, dear teachers, parents! - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Dear graduates, dear teachers, parents!
content:
Dear students, this day symbolizes your hard work, patience and pursuit of knowledge. You have traveled a difficult path full of challenges, discoveries and personal growth. Now it is time to reap the fruits of your efforts. You are ready to apply the knowledge and skills you have gained to reach new horizons and build your own future.

For each of you, university has been not only a place of learning, but also a space for making friends and creating valuable connections with mentors. In the course of your studies, you have learned not only professional skills, but also how to adapt to change, work in teams, take responsibility and form as individuals ready for independent achievements and creative discoveries.

However, today is not the end, but only the beginning of a new journey. The world beyond our University opens up new opportunities and challenges for you. There will be both successes and challenges ahead, but I am confident that the knowledge and experience you have gained here will be a reliable support on your journey.

Let me express my gratitude to our faculty and staff who have generously shared their knowledge, patience, and warmth of heart with you. A special thanks to your parents and loved ones who have supported you throughout this journey. It is your parents’ faith in you that has allowed us to gather here today. Your achievements are also their merit.

Each of you is unique and each of you has your own future ahead of you. Remember that education is not the final goal, but the starting point to new heights.

Don’t forget our University, because it will always be a part of your history, just as you are a part of ours. We are proud of you and we are sure that you will make a great contribution to the development of our country!

title: The “Rector’s Olympiad” was organized by the Tashkent International University of Education. - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: The “Rector’s Olympiad” was organized by the Tashkent International University of Education.
content:
As part of the collaboration between the Tashkent International University of Education and the Tashkent City Department of Preschool and School Education, the “Rector’s Olympiad” was held from March to May of this year. The initiative aimed to support and encourage talented school students in foreign languages and exact sciences.

A total of 7290 11th-grade students participated in the first stage of the Olympiad. All participants were awarded scholarships of up to 1,800,000 UZS to study at the university, while 902 top-performing students in the first stage received educational grants of up to 9,000,000 UZS.

The final stage of the Olympiad took place on May 21–22 at TIUE and focused on the subjects of English and mathematics. During the final round, students showcased their knowledge, analytical thinking, and creativity. The most outstanding participants in English and mathematics were declared winners.

The award ceremony is scheduled to be held on May 24 during the “Last Bell” event at the winners’ respective schools. During the ceremony, Olympiad winners will be granted educational scholarships of up to 21,000,000 UZS and valuable prizes. In addition, commemorative gifts will be presented to their subject teachers.

Below is the list of winners of the “Rector’s Olympiad” organized by the Tashkent International University of Education, along with students recommended for university admission.

Results of the 2nd (final) stage of the “Rector’s Olympiad” of the International University.

title: TIUE на Международной образовательной конференции: новые горизонты сотрудничества! - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE на Международной образовательной конференции: новые горизонты сотрудничества!
content:
TIUE на Международной образовательной конференции: новые горизонты сотрудничества!

Сегодня ректор Tashkent International University of Education Ходжаев Носир Нодирович и проректор Байджанов Максудбек Исламджанович приняли участие в международной конференции, посвященной обмену знаниями и опыту в сфере образования. Основной целью мероприятия стало обсуждение актуальных проблем и поиск новых решений, а также создание основ для международного сотрудничества.

В конференции участвовали более 100 экспертов, включая профессоров Гронингенского университета в Нидерландах, заместителя директора Шанхайского центра подготовки учителей ЮНЕСКО, представителей образовательных учреждений из Сингапура и Шанхая, а также местных руководителей и преподавателей.

Это событие стало важным шагом в развитии образовательной среды и выявлении специалистов, готовых внести вклад в будущее образования.

title: Higher education in Uzbekistan: what you need to know
meta_description: Let's consider which to choose: state or private higher education in Tashkent. Let's consider all the advantages, features, and prospects of graduates. Learn what advantages and disadvantages Uzbekistan's higher education institutions have to make the right decision!
content:
The higher education system of Uzbekistan performs an important function of training professional personnel ready to work effectively in various sectors of the national economy and scientific activity. The educational structure of the republic includes three categories of higher educational institutions: state universities, branches of foreign universities, and private universities. Each of these categories is characterized by specific features and features of functioning.

The higher education system in the Republic of Uzbekistan has many features that are important to consider when choosing a university. The country has a wide representation of state-funded higher education institutions, as well as branches of foreign universities and private universities. Therefore, there are great prospects for applicants in terms of choosing a suitable higher education.

- State funding – most higher education institutions receive support from the state, which affects the cost of education and the quality of educational programs.

- Wide choice of educational directions – various specialties are offered in government institutions, branches of foreign universities, and private universities, which allows students to find their suitable field of study.

- Mandatory state licensing and accreditation – all educational institutions must comply with the quality standards established by the state.

- The high level of control by state bodies contributes to maintaining a stable level of education and programs implemented by each university in the Republic of Uzbekistan.

- The presence of international programs – both state and private universities are implementing educational programs with international accreditation, which expands students’ opportunities to study abroad.

- Development of the material and technical base – in recent years, Uzbekistan has been actively investing in the state and private systems of educational institutions, creating comfortable conditions for studying and scientific work.

These features form the basis of the education system in the Republic of Uzbekistan, making it modern and compliant with international standards. They allow creating optimal conditions for applicants and students in each educational institution representing both the state and private sectors.

The choice between state, foreign, and private universities depends on many factors: the student’s goals, financial capabilities, and preferences in studying. Education in the Republic of Uzbekistan continues to develop, and all universities strive to improve the quality of the educational process. Ultimately, it is important not only the name of the institution, but also the acquired knowledge, skills, and experience that will help the graduate successfully realize themselves in the professional field.

title: How to get into a foreign university from Uzbekistan: preparation for bachelor's degree abroad
meta_description: Learn how to enter a foreign university from Uzbekistan and what steps are necessary for a successful start. We will talk about education abroad, the Foundation program, and the specifics of higher education in Great Britain. Get ready to study abroad and choose International Foundation Program
content:
Obtaining higher education abroad has long been the goal of many young people striving for quality education and a promising career. Today, opportunities are more open than ever: in-demand and high-quality programs, accessible learning formats, flexible conditions, and a high level of student preparation attract thousands of applicants from around the world.

Uzbekistan’s youth are particularly active in showing interest in foreign universities, which is explained by their desire to go abroad, receive international-level education, and be competitive in the global labor market.

One of the popular ways to enter a foreign university while traveling abroad is through the IFP (International Foundation Program) special preparatory program. This program allows you to go abroad not only to further study abroad, but also to adapt to the foreign academic environment, acquire the necessary knowledge, and significantly improve your foreign language proficiency. In addition, the IFP program helps future students understand the requirements of higher education institutions abroad and confidently receive education abroad.

The IFP program is designed for students wishing to continue their studies abroad in countries with an English-language education system. As a link between universities of the Republic of Uzbekistan and foreign universities, the IFP program provides fundamental academic and language training. Usually, the program is designed for one academic year and includes courses in English, mathematics, academic writing, as well as specialized disciplines depending on the chosen specialty.

The advantages of studying abroad at prestigious universities for students from the Republic of Uzbekistan are difficult to overestimate:

- High quality of education, as foreign universities combine centuries-old academic traditions with advanced teaching approaches

- An international learning environment that allows you to broaden your horizons, acquire new perspectives and connections.

- Modern educational technologies, as programs include digital tools, interactive formats, and applied methods, bringing learning closer to real-world tasks.

- Intensive language practice, immersion in the language environment, which contributes to the rapid and sustainable growth of language skills.

- Access to grants and scholarships offered by numerous universities to foreign students in the form of full or partial funding.

- Competitiveness in the labor market, because graduates of foreign universities are in high demand in international and national companies.

This path allows the student not only to acquire quality knowledge but also to develop the necessary skills for a successful career abroad.

Among the most popular destinations for Uzbek students traveling abroad, the following

Next, let’s consider the specifics of each of the listed countries.

- Great Britain is traditionally among the leaders in the field of higher education. There is an IFP program system here that allows you to prepare for a bachelor’s degree. Teaching is conducted in English, and students from Uzbekistan can choose programs from many fields.

- Germany and Italy are attractive in that they provide free or inexpensive education even for foreign students. Strong engineering and economics schools make it particularly attractive for admission.

- South Korea is known for its high-tech universities and a modern approach to teaching. This direction is especially interesting for students who want to work in IT and innovation.

- Turkey and Poland offer affordable tuition fees and good living conditions. In addition, these countries have many agreements with universities in Europe and Asia, which opens up additional prospects.

- The USA and Malaysia provide a variety of programs, a high level of academic training, and modern campuses. Programs for traveling abroad for the purpose of receiving education there are built according to a model focused on soft skills development.

Each country offers unique conditions not only for quality education but also for career development, allowing you to later go abroad for work or successfully find employment in Uzbekistan.

- Entering a foreign university requires thorough preparation from young people. Below are the main steps that Uzbek applicants should consider:

- Determine which direction and which country is most suitable for you in terms of conditions, cost, and prospects.

- Pay attention to the university’s position in world rankings.

- Find a university that offers a program that interests you and corresponds to your academic level.

- Preparing documents such as certificates, letters of recommendation, motivational letters, language certificates (for example, IELTS or TOEFL).

- Submission of an application. Fill out online applications through the university’s official website or with the help of an agent.

- Undergoing preparation. If necessary, you can go through the IFP program or similar preparatory program.

- Obtaining a visa. After enrollment, it is necessary to obtain a student visa at the embassy or consulate of the chosen country.

- Preparation for departure. Solve housing, health insurance, finance, and transportation issues.

Following these steps coming from Uzbekistan, it becomes much easier to adapt to the requirements of foreign universities and successfully start your studies.

TIUE, as an international university, offers students from Uzbekistan the opportunity to start

their journey to foreign education within their own country. The International Foundation Program (IFP) implemented at our university is designed specifically for those young people who aspire to study abroad. We provide a high level of academic training, adapted to international standards, and help students enter the world’s leading universities.

Thanks to such a program and the support of experienced specialists, the path to foreign education becomes easier, and the chances of enrollment are significantly higher. We are confident that with proper preparation and strong motivation, every student will be able to receive their dream education abroad, for which no border will be an obstacle.

Students who successfully complete the program will receive the “OTHM Level 3 Foundation Diploma for Higher Education Studies” diploma, which will allow them to continue their studies at the bachelor’s level in both Uzbekistan’s higher education institutions and foreign universities.

title: How to choose a university in Tashkent? Tips for choosing the right university
meta_description: Learn how to choose a university for admission to Tashkent. We will tell you what to pay attention to when choosing a university, what criteria to consider, and how to choose a university correctly for successful and promising learning. Get useful advice and examples to make a decision!
content:
Every summer for hundreds of thousands of Uzbek school graduates comes a tense and responsible period. The completion of the academic year brings not only joy but also an important choice – which university to choose to receive a quality education and lay a solid foundation for a successful career. For an applicant, making the right decision at that moment is one of the most significant steps in life. Therefore, it is important to understand in advance the many offered areas of training, the quality of education provided, the cost of the contract for training, and further employment prospects and the demand for the chosen profession in the future.

The education system in the country includes hundreds of universities, both old and new. Both state and private universities, as well as branches of foreign universities, offer a diverse choice of higher education. Choosing a suitable option depends on each applicant’s goals, level of preparation, and priorities.

Before starting to make a choice, we recommend familiarizing yourself with the general features of the higher education system of Uzbekistan, and only then proceed to making a decision:

- Mulk form diversity, which is expressed in the fact that both state and private universities operate in the Republic of Uzbekistan, offering different programs and approaches to teaching.

- Development of international cooperation, as many universities cooperate with foreign partners, which allows students to participate in academic mobility and exchange programs.

- The diversity of specialties, as every year an applicant can choose from hundreds of fields in various fields: from medicine and jurisprudence to IT and business.

- Growth in the number of private universities, as the number of such universities has increased significantly in recent years, especially in major cities like Tashkent.

- Improving the quality of education, Uzbekistan strives to adapt to international standards, and universities are implementing modern teaching and assessment methods.

Thus, Uzbekistan’s universities are becoming increasingly accessible, diverse, and attractive for young people. But at the same time, every applicant has a question – which university to choose and where to start?

Entering state universities requires an applicant to understand the algorithm and complete all steps in a timely manner. The system of admission to state universities in Uzbekistan is based on the principle of selection through a single test. The main features are the centralized submission of applications, standardized testing, strict rating selection, and a system of privileges for certain categories of applicants.

It’s worth noting that the specific details of the procedure may vary from year to year, so applicants are advised to follow the current information on the official websites of the Ministry of Higher Education, Science and Innovation of the Republic of Uzbekistan and their chosen universities.

Enrollment in branches of foreign universities and private universities

Admission to such universities in Uzbekistan is carried out according to somewhat different principles compared to state universities. These educational institutions typically establish their own selection criteria for applicants, which may include not only state test results but also additional exams, interviews, achievement portfolios, or language certificates such as IELTS or TOEFL.

Foreign university branches often require compliance with international academic standards, while private universities can provide more flexible admission conditions considering each candidate’s individual characteristics and potential. At the same time, training in such institutions is predominantly paid, which is compensated by the modern material and technical base, the optimal number of groups, and the possibility of obtaining diplomas of both state and international standards.

Despite the fact that in each specific case, there are peculiarities in choosing a university, the general mechanism remains similar.

Many applicants face a difficult choice between public and private universities. Which option is better for an Uzbek graduate? There is no universal answer to this.

Each university has its advantages, some of which are attractive due to stability, support from the state, and a wide network of branches, while others, in turn, are oriented towards practicality, novelty of approaches, and integration with the business environment.

Therefore, choose your option based on your goals and expectations. To make it easier to determine the university, we recommend analyzing the provided program in detail and learning about the learning conditions.

Advantages of the Tashkent International University of Education

One of the bright examples of a successful private university is the Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE). It offers unique programs and approaches that meet international standards and the needs of the modern student. TIUE University’s advantages are as follows:

- International approach, as TIUE University implements international educational programs and cooperates with various foreign partners (Great Britain, Malaysia, South Korea), which allows students to broaden their horizons for the future.

- Modern infrastructure, the university is located in Tashkent and offers a comfortable, technological learning environment.

- Individual approach, each student has access to personal academic support and consultations.

- Career orientation, as TIUE University actively interacts with companies and helps students find work during their studies.

- Academic freedom implemented through flexible programs, the ability to choose courses and formats – all of this makes the learning process more interesting.

By choosing TIUE University, you get not just a higher education diploma, but access to quality education and international opportunities. In the context of the increasing number of higher education institutions in the Republic of Uzbekistan, it is important not only to choose a university but also to evaluate all the prospects it offers. Choose consciously – and let your decision be the first step towards success. Having chosen the optimal option for yourself, start preparing, and let our recommendations be as beneficial as possible for you.

title: What directions are the most demanded in universities of Uzbekistan: where to enter in 2025?
meta_description: Find out what are the most in-demand fields of study at universities in Uzbekistan and where to enrol in 2025. We will tell you about popular specialities in universities, promising humanitarian and technical directions, as well as give advice on choosing a faculty in Tashkent. Choose the best areas of study in universities to ensure a successful future!
content:
Choosing a university is one of the most important stages in the life of every applicant. Not only his further education, but also his professional trajectory depends on him. In 2025, the number of programs and specialties offered by universities in Tashkent and other regions of Uzbekistan continues to grow, which expands the possibilities for choosing, but at the same time complicates this procedure. However, if you approach the issue systematically, find a suitable direction and determine where to apply for admission, it’s quite realistic.

Modern applicants are increasingly focusing on practicality and employment prospects when choosing a profession. When choosing universities, both classical and innovative specialties are still in demand. If we talk about the most popular professions, directions, and, accordingly, the faculties that applicants in 2025 aspire to enter, we can highlight the following:

- Economics. A popular profession with a wide range of applications in the private and public sectors. As a rule, competition is very high in economics faculties. However, a large number of offers (mastering this profession is offered in almost every major university or other type of university) simplifies the search for future economists.

- Jurisprudence. The direction is in demand in administrative, corporate, and international structures. By choosing a profession related to jurisprudence, applicants open a direct path to prestigious and high-paying specialties.

- Information technology. A direction that combines programming, cybersecurity, and software development. Choosing a university with the opportunity to study such specialties is a real opportunity to successfully find employment in both the best companies in the country and in foreign firms and organizations.

- Pedagogy. The most stable humanitarian direction in the education system, which trains future teachers for preschool institutions, schools, lyceums, and technical schools.

- Medicine. The most competitive direction, requiring high preparation and motivation.

- Architecture. A direction that connects creativity and technical knowledge.

- International relations. A specialization focused on diplomacy and strategic analysis at the global level.

- Engineering technologies. They are in demand in industrial enterprises and scientific institutes.

These directions in universities cover a wide range of professions and allow you to choose the trajectory of development according to your interests. Uzbekistan is actively investing in the education system, therefore the quality of programs offered by each university and individual faculty continues to grow.

The humanities faculties in Tashkent remain attractive due to their versatility and flexibility in applying knowledge. Thousands of applicants prefer this direction. Below are the most popular specialties in this field:

- Journalism. Training of specialists in media, PR, and digital media. This, as is generally accepted, is the oldest profession, requiring language proficiency, analytical thinking, and flexibility. Moreover, journalism is a prestigious field, as it opens up good prospects for working in an international format.

- Psychology. Fundamentals of the theory and practice of psychodiagnostics, counseling, and work in educational institutions. The direction is in demand in educational, medical, and HR structures.

- Linguistics. Studying languages, translations, intercultural communication. A good choice for future translators, teachers, and editors.

- History. Fundamental knowledge about the development of society, the state, and international processes. A university specializing in history provides a broad base for scientific, pedagogical, and museum activities.

- Philology. Deep study of language, literature, literary studies, and linguistics. A popular direction among those who want to work in the field of education and translation.

Humanities require a high level of language proficiency and analytical skills. They are attractive to students seeking to teach, research, and engage in cultural activities. Almost every university, even if it’s not of a humanities profile, has such a faculty or several.

Technical education in Uzbekistan remains in demand due to the development of infrastructure, industry, and digital technologies. The most popular industries here are:

- Automation and control. It is used in industry, logistics, and robotics. One of the most promising areas in mechanical engineering and production.

- Computer Sciences. Focus on IT development, algorithms, and information security. Includes programming, databases, machine learning.

- Electric power industry. Specialists are engaged in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. A classical engineering specialty that is in demand in various spheres and industries.

- Architectural design. It combines technical and artistic aspects of design. It is in demand in construction and urban planning.

- Mathematics and Informatics. Advanced study of algorithms, analytics, and modeling. Fundamentals for scientific and research activities applied in education, even if the university is not technical.

These specialties open the way for high-paying and promising professions. At the same time, it is important to consider personal abilities and interest in exact sciences.

Universities of Uzbekistan: where to enter in 2025

The choice of a university depends on the direction, conditions, and quality of training. In the Republic of Uzbekistan, there are a wide range of universities with various specializations:

- National University of Uzbekistan. One of the oldest universities, it includes a large number of faculties. It has a developed scientific base and extensive research opportunities.

- University of World Economy and Diplomacy. Suitable for those planning a career in international politics and business. Graduates of this educational institution often hold high positions in government and private structures.

- Tashkent State Technical University. He specializes in technical specialties. The university actively cooperates with industrial enterprises and develops engineering programs.

- Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE). A private university in Tashkent offering programs in English and practice-oriented education. Suitable for students focused on international standards and a flexible learning approach.

- Uzbekistan State World Languages University. A leading university for training linguists and translators. Here, the linguistic foundation for future teachers and communication specialists is formed.

- Tashkent Medical University. The best choice for those who want to work in the medical field.

- Tashkent University of Architecture and Civil Engineering. A specialized university in the fields of design and engineering. Students are actively involved in applied projects and urban planning.

Admission to a university in Tashkent in 2025 requires a responsible approach. Future applicants should study the directions, faculties, bachelor’s programs, in-demand specialists, and admission conditions in advance. To make a conscious choice, it is useful to compare specialties, study the list of higher educational institutions, find out the passing score, and then apply to the chosen higher educational institution.

If you haven’t yet decided where to apply, read the information in this article again. Although you have to decide for yourself, such data can help you make a decision. Choose, prepare, apply, and receive higher education at the best universities in the country!
