title: How to enrol in a foreign university from Uzbekistan: preparing for a bachelor's degree abroad
meta_description: Find out how to enrol in a foreign university from Uzbekistan and what steps are necessary for a successful start. We will tell you about education abroad, the Foundation programme and the features of higher education in the United Kingdom. Prepare for studying abroad and choose the International Foundation Programme with TIUE!
content:
Getting a higher education abroad has long been a goal for many young people who want quality knowledge and a promising career. Today, the opportunities are more open than ever: sought-after, high-quality programmes, accessible learning formats, flexible conditions, and a high level of student training attract thousands of applicants from all over the world. Young people from Uzbekistan are particularly interested in foreign universities, which is explained by their desire to go abroad, receive an international-level education and be competitive in the global labour market.

One of the most popular ways to enrol in a foreign university abroad is to complete a special preparatory programme called IFP (International Foundation Programme). This programme allows you not only to go abroad for further study, but also to adapt to the foreign academic environment, brush up on the necessary knowledge and significantly improve your foreign language skills. In addition, the IFP programme helps prospective students understand the requirements of higher education institutions abroad and confidently pursue their education abroad.

The IFP programme is designed for students who wish to continue their studies abroad in countries with an English-language education system. Acting as a link between universities in the Republic of Uzbekistan and foreign universities, the IFP programme provides fundamental academic and language training. The programme usually lasts one academic year and includes courses in English, mathematics, academic writing, and subject-specific disciplines depending on the chosen specialisation.

The advantages of studying abroad at prestigious universities for students from the Republic of Uzbekistan cannot be overstated:

- High quality education, as foreign universities combine centuries-old academic traditions with cutting-edge approaches to teaching.

- Intensive language practice and immersion in the language environment promote rapid and sustained growth in language skills.

- Access to grants and scholarships offered to international students by many universities in the form of full or partial funding.

This path allows students not only to gain high-quality knowledge, but also to develop the skills necessary for a successful career abroad.

Among the most popular destinations for students from Uzbekistan going abroad, the following countries can be highlighted:

Let us consider the characteristics of each of the countries listed above.

- The United Kingdom has traditionally been among the leaders in higher education. It has a system of IFP programmes that allow students to prepare for a bachelor’s degree. Teaching is conducted in English, and students from Uzbekistan can choose from a wide range of programmes.

- Germany and Italy are attractive because they offer free or inexpensive education even for foreign students. Strong engineering and economics schools make them particularly appealing for admission.

- South Korea is renowned for its high-tech universities and modern approach to education. This destination is particularly appealing to students seeking careers in IT and innovation.

- The United States and Malaysia offer a variety of programmes, a high level of academic training and modern campuses. Programmes for studying abroad are modelled on the development of soft skills.

Each country offers unique conditions not only for high-quality education, but also for career development, allowing you to subsequently go abroad to work or find successful employment in Uzbekistan.

How to enrol in a university abroad from Uzbekistan

Enrolling in a foreign university requires thorough preparation on the part of young people. Below are the main steps that applicants from Uzbekistan should consider:

- Please note the university’s position in global rankings.

- Find a university that offers the programme you are interested in and matches your academic level.

- Preparation of documents such as certificates, letters of recommendation, motivation letters, language certificates (e.g. IELTS or TOEFL).

- Submitting an application. Complete the online application form via the university’s official website or with the help of an agent.

- Completion of training. If necessary, you can complete the IFP programme or a similar preparatory programme.

- Obtaining a visa. After enrolment, you must apply for a student visa at the embassy or consulate of your chosen country.

- Prepare for departure. Sort out accommodation, medical insurance, finances and transport.

As an international university, TIUE offers students from Uzbekistan the opportunity to begin their journey towards foreign education while still in their home country. The International Foundation Programme (IFP) offered at our university is designed specifically for young people who wish to study abroad. We provide a high level of academic training adapted to international standards and help students gain admission to the world’s leading universities.

Thanks to this programme and the support of experienced specialists, the path to foreign education becomes easier, and the chances of admission are significantly higher. We are confident that with the right preparation and strong motivation, every student will be able to get the education of their dreams abroad, for which no border will be an obstacle.

Students who successfully complete the programme will receive an OTHM Level 3 Foundation Diploma for Higher Education Studies, which will allow them to continue their studies at bachelor’s level at both higher education institutions in Uzbekistan and universities abroad.

title: For Students - Tashkent International University of Education
content:
We’re thrilled to have you as a part of our university community. This page is dedicated to providing you with all the information you need to make the most out of your time with us. From important dates and deadlines to course schedules and campus events, you’ll find everything you need to know right here.

Our Student Services Department is here to help you with any questions or concerns you may have during your time at our university. We understand that navigating academic and personal challenges can be tough, but you don’t have to go through it alone. Our team of dedicated professionals is here to support you every step of the way.

Our Student Services Department offers assistance in a variety of areas, including:

Academic advising: Our advisors are available to help you plan your course schedule, choose a major, and navigate academic requirements.

Career services: Our career services team offers resources and guidance to help you explore career options, search for internships and job opportunities, and prepare for interviews.Counseling services: Our licensed counselors are available to provide confidential support for a range of personal concerns, including stress, anxiety, depression, and relationship issues. Disability services: We offer accommodations and support services for students with disabilities to ensure equal access to education and campus activities.

Financial aid: Our financial aid advisors can help you navigate the financial aid process, including applying for scholarships and loans.

Our Student Services Department is available during regular business hours, and we also offer virtual appointments for your convenience. To make an appointment or to learn more about our services, please visit our website or stop by our office in person.

We encourage you to take advantage of our Student Services Department throughout your time at our university. We’re here to support you and help you succeed in all areas of your academic and personal life.

Our university operates on a semester-based system, with Fall and Spring semesters running from late October to January and late January to May, respectively.

You can view your personalized course timetable by navigating to tiue.edupage.org. Here, you’ll be able to see all of your course schedules, including lecture times, lab sessions, and tutorials.

We encourage you to check your timetable regularly, as changes may be made throughout the semester.

Our university offers a wide range of programs and courses to suit every interest and career goal in such areas as information technologies, business management, education and training management.

Our curriculum is designed to provide you with a well-rounded education that prepares you for success in your chosen field. We offer a mix of core courses and electives, allowing you to tailor your degree to your interests and career aspirations. Our experienced faculty members are experts in their respective fields and are dedicated to helping you achieve your academic goals.

You can receive information on curriculum of programs here:

It’s important to stay on top of important dates and deadlines throughout the semester. Here are a few key dates to keep in mind:

Last Day to Add/Drop Courses: Second week of the semester

Midterm Exams: Mid-October (Fall) and Late February (Spring)

The University has several modern computer labs, which students can use during the classes or after classes.

The University’s security team is available to help 24 hours a day, seven days a week, throughout the whole year, at the campus.

The health of students is important for Tashkent International University of Education. Taking into account this, the university has a Medical Center.

meta_description: Exciting Opportunity for TIUE Students
content:
Are you harboring a business idea but feel uncertain about how to kick-start it or lack the initial capital? Or perhaps you’re contemplating launching a business in the future? We encourage you to participate in a business accelerator program for startup projects organized by the Fintech Association of Uzbekistan.

Supported by The Stirling Foundation (USA), this project aims to nurture startup ventures in the fields of EdTech, FinTech, CleanTech, and ClimateTech. As part of this initiative, five finalists will be selected, each standing a chance to win financial prizes ranging from 1.5 to 15 thousand US dollars.

The highlight of this endeavor, the “Pitch Day” will take place on April 25, 2024, at 9:30 am, hosted by the British Management University. This event will feature participation from international investors, presenting an invaluable opportunity for networking and exposure to potential backers.

Furthermore, we invite you to attend the event as guests, providing you with valuable insights and experience for your future entrepreneurial endeavors.

Don’t miss out on this incredible chance to turn your business dreams into reality!

title: "Tashkent International University of Education" Rector's Olympiad - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: "Tashkent International University of Education" Rector's Olympiad
content:
On March 5-6, 2024, within the framework of the cooperation between Tashkent International University of Education and the Tashkent City Department of Pre-school and School Education, general education schools in order to identify talented young people, support them and train highly qualified personnel “Tashkent International University of Education Rector’s Olympiad” was organized among students of the graduating class.

201 11th-grade students from general schools in Tashkent will participate in the Olympiad and test their knowledge of mathematics and English.

According to the final results of the Olympiad, the winners are encouraged in the following order:

For the 1st place – a laptop and a 100% grant for studying at the university for one academic year;

For the 2nd place – a tablet and a 75% grant for studying at the university in one academic year;

For the 3rd place – a mobile phone and a 50% grant for studying at the university for one academic year;

For 4th-14th places – souvenirs of the university and a 30% grant for studying in one academic year;

For the 15th-25th places – souvenirs of the university and a 20% grant for studying in one academic year are provided.

All winners (ranked 1-25) will be admitted to TIUE International University without entrance exams.

Note: According to the Olympic Regulations, in the event that several participants have equal points for the winning places according to the final results, the participant with the highest score on the test results in mathematics will have priority. if the test results are also equal, the student who scored more points on complex test tasks will have an advantage.

Dear participants, “Tashkent International University of Education” thanks you all for participating in the Olympiad, and our team wishes you more success in your studies, striving for great achievements in life, achieving your goals, gaining new knowledge and increasing your professional experience.

*Furthermore, the University administration is pleased to announce that it is ready to accept students with 50 points and above without entrance exams to Tashkent International University of Education, respecting the high knowledge and intellectual potential of all participants. !

You can get acquainted with the results of the Olympics through the link below.

For additional information, you can contact the Organizing Committee by phone number +99 890 933-30-23.

title: On April 17, 2024, a number of events were held at Tashkent International University of Education in connection with Nowruz holiday. - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: On April 17, 2024, a number of events were held at Tashkent International University of Education in connection with Nowruz holiday.
content:
The job fair organized especially for graduates was a great success, prestigious companies from various sectors of Uzbekistan took part in the event. The participants had the opportunity to communicate with representatives of well-known banks, communication companies and large supermarket chains such as “Korzinka Business”. In addition, state and non-state organizations such as Uzcard, Coca-Cola, Artel, Huawei, Kapital, TBC, Silk Road Bank, Riks Education took an active part in recruiting talented individuals from among graduates. During the fair, these prestigious employer companies presented interesting job offers to the graduates, showing them a wide range of job opportunities in various fields. Graduates had the opportunity to learn about company culture, job responsibilities and development prospects, which enabled them to make informed decisions about their future careers.

In addition, the book fair organized for students was held with the participation of famous bookstores such as “Book in Dom”, “Topar.uz”, “Asahiy”. Students had the opportunity to study a variety of books, from academic textbooks to fiction and personal development.

In addition, various sports games such as football, basketball, table tennis, tug-of-war and shuttlecock were held among the students. The participants and guests enjoyed various entertainment programs, national dishes of various times, presentation of attractive national costumes and performance of national songs and dances.

These events were a true reflection of the spirit of Nowruz, promoting unity, diversity and joy at Tashkent International University of Education.

title: Dear full-time students - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Dear full-time students
content:
🔰 All students must take part in exams at the time specified in the exam schedule.

🔰 During the exams, students are required to strictly follow the rules of discipline and order. Before taking the exam, students are required to present their identity document (student ID card).

🔰 Below we mention the instructions for passing written exams.

✅ Arrive at the auditorium 20 minutes before the start of the exam. In case of lateness, the student will not be admitted to the exam;

✅Providing an original copy of the student ID card or Passport confirming identity;

✅ Bring a pen, pencil and calculator to the math exam (if allowed by the rules);

Carefully check the auditorium number and seat number assigned to each student;

✅ Before entering the exam hall, students must be ready to be checked for the presence of mats or items that are not allowed to be used during the exam;

✅After being allowed to enter the auditorium, students must take their seats and wait for further instructions from the invigilators (representatives in charge of the university) and not touch the question paper before the exams begin.

🔴 Phone/smart watch, gadgets or bag. If there is, you must leave the items on the list in the audience.

🔴 Sheets, notebooks or other foreign objects, if not specified in the instructions.

Carefully fill in your information on the exam answer sheet displayed on the student’s desk and check everything by passport and/or ID card and index-number.

🔴 During the exam, it is forbidden to communicate with students, look around, talk or disturb others, violate the rules of discipline and order.

🔴 If you have any questions, raise your hand and wait for the invigilator to come to you.

When the exam time is over, stop writing and hand in the work to the invigilator as required by the instructions.

🔴 During the exam, if the student has not submitted the work, it is forbidden to leave the auditorium

📞 If you have additional questions, you can contact the Department of Work with Students (room 113) or the Examination Department (room 102).

title: How to choose a university in Tashkent? Tips for choosing the right university
meta_description: Learn how to choose a university for admission to Tashkent. We will tell you what to pay attention to when choosing a university, what criteria to consider, and how to choose a university correctly for successful and promising learning. Get useful advice and examples to make a decision!
content:
Every summer for hundreds of thousands of Uzbek school graduates comes a tense and responsible period. The completion of the academic year brings not only joy but also an important choice – which university to choose to receive a quality education and lay a solid foundation for a successful career. For an applicant, making the right decision at that moment is one of the most significant steps in life. Therefore, it is important to understand in advance the many offered areas of training, the quality of education provided, the cost of the contract for training, and further employment prospects and the demand for the chosen profession in the future.

The education system in the country includes hundreds of universities, both old and new. Both state and private universities, as well as branches of foreign universities, offer a diverse choice of higher education. Choosing a suitable option depends on each applicant’s goals, level of preparation, and priorities.

Before starting to make a choice, we recommend familiarizing yourself with the general features of the higher education system of Uzbekistan, and only then proceed to making a decision:

- The diversity of ownership forms, which is expressed in the fact that both state and private universities operate in the Republic of Uzbekistan, offering different programs and approaches to teaching.

- Development of international cooperation, as many universities cooperate with foreign partners, which allows students to participate in academic mobility and exchange programs.

- The diversity of specialties, as every year an applicant can choose from hundreds of fields in various fields: from medicine and jurisprudence to IT and business.

- The growth of private universities, as the number of such universities has increased significantly in recent years, especially in major cities like Tashkent.

- Improving the quality of education, Uzbekistan strives to adapt to international standards, and universities are implementing modern teaching and assessment methods.

Thus, Uzbekistan’s universities are becoming increasingly accessible, diverse, and attractive for young people. But at the same time, every applicant has a question – which university to choose and where to start?

Entering state universities requires an applicant to understand the algorithm and complete all steps in a timely manner. The system of admission to state universities in Uzbekistan is based on the principle of selection through a single test. The main features are the centralized submission of applications, standardized testing, strict rating selection, and a system of privileges for certain categories of applicants.

It’s worth noting that the specific details of the procedure may vary from year to year, so applicants are advised to follow the current information on the official websites of the Ministry of Higher Education, Science and Innovation of the Republic of Uzbekistan and their chosen universities.

Enrollment in branches of foreign universities and private universities

Admission to such universities in Uzbekistan is carried out according to somewhat different principles compared to state universities. These educational institutions typically establish their own selection criteria for applicants, which may include not only state test results but also additional exams, interviews, achievement portfolios, or language certificates such as IELTS or TOEFL.

Foreign university branches often require compliance with international academic standards, while private universities can provide more flexible admission conditions considering each candidate’s individual characteristics and potential. At the same time, training in such institutions is predominantly paid, which is compensated by the modern material and technical base, the optimal number of groups, and the possibility of obtaining diplomas of both state and international standards.

Despite the fact that in each specific case, there are peculiarities in choosing a university, the general mechanism remains similar.

Many applicants face a difficult choice between public and private universities. Which option is better for an Uzbek graduate? There is no universal answer to this.

Each university has its advantages, some of which are attractive due to stability, support from the state, and a wide network of branches, while others, in turn, are oriented towards practicality, novelty of approaches, and integration with the business environment. Therefore, choose your option based on your goals and expectations. To make it easier to determine the university, we recommend analyzing the provided program in detail and learning about the learning conditions.

Advantages of the Tashkent International University of Education

One striking example of a successful private university is Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE). It offers unique programmes and approaches that meet international standards and the needs of modern students.

- International approach, as TIUE University implements international educational programmes and cooperates with various foreign partners (the United Kingdom, Malaysia, South Korea), which allows students to broaden their horizons for the future.

- With modern infrastructure, the university is located in Tashkent and offers a comfortable, technologically advanced environment for study.

- An individual approach, with personal academic support and advice available to every student.

- Career focus, as TIUE actively interacts with companies and helps students find jobs while still studying.

- Academic freedom, which is achieved through flexible programmes and the ability to choose courses and formats, makes the learning process more interesting.

By choosing TIUE, you get not just a higher education diploma, but access to quality education and international opportunities. With the number of higher education institutions in the Republic of Uzbekistan growing, it is important not only to choose a university, but also to evaluate all the prospects it offers. Make an informed choice — and let your decision be the first step to success. Once you have chosen the best option for you, start preparing, and may our recommendations be of maximum benefit to you.

title: 30 ноября текущего года в международном университете "Tashkent International University of Education" - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: 30 ноября текущего года в международном университете "Tashkent International University of Education"
content:
30 ноября текущего года в международном университете “Tashkent International University of Education” состоялся семинар-тренинг по системе образования Сингапура

В семинаре-тренинге приняли участие магистранты по специальностям «История и теория педагогики» и «Управление образовательных учреждений».

Также в процессе семинара-тренинга, обсуждениях и практических заданиях активно участвовали преподаватели университета.

Особенности семинара состояли в том, что был применены интерактивный формат обсуждения особенностей сингапурской модели образования, выполнялись практические задания для понимания современных подходов и методов, а также происходил живой обмен мнениями и опытом.

Ваш интерес и вовлеченность сделали семинар незабываемым!

Следите за нашими новостями – впереди ещё больше интересных мероприятий.

title: TIUE students visited "Alpha Kids" - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE students visited "Alpha Kids"
content:
On 14 November, students of the undergraduate program “Management of Education and educational processes” of “Tashkent International University of Education” (TIUE) visited the non-governmental educational institution of preschool education “Alpha Kids” as part of a field-trip.

Students attended a number of classes held for children in this institution. In particular, they attended English classes, a speech therapist’s office, as well as extracurricular classes. It is important to note that Alpha Kids also conducts preparatory classes for preschool children, classes in rhythmic gymnastics, taekwondo, etc.

Students also visited each study group and had the opportunity to directly observe the educational process. As a result of this field-trip, students gained practical knowledge and skills in working in the field of preschool education, which is directly related to their future professional activities.

Organizer of the field-trip: lecturer of the Department “Management of Education and educational processes”, PhD D.Rakhimdzhanova.

title: November 18 – the day of adoption of the state flag of Uzbekistan - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: November 18 – the day of adoption of the state flag of Uzbekistan
content:
The Law “On the State Flag of the Republic of Uzbekistan” was adopted on November 18, 1991 at the eighth session of the Supreme Council of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

The symbolism of the State flag of the Republic of Uzbekistan continues the best traditions inherent in the flags of powerful powers that existed on the territory of our country, at the same time reflects the natural features of the republic, the national and cultural identity of the people.

Sky blue on the flag is a symbol of blue sky and clear water. The azure color is revered in the East; the great Amir Temur once chose it for his flag.

White color is a symbol of peace and purity. A young independent state must overcome high passes on its way. The white color on the flag means a good wish that the path is clean and bright.

Green color is the personification of blessed nature. Currently, the movement to protect the environment is expanding around the world, the symbol of which is also the green color.

Red stripes are the life forces pulsating in every living creature, a symbol of life.

The crescent corresponds to the centuries-old tradition of the people of Uzbekistan. The crescent and stars are a symbol of the cloudless sky of the world. Our flag has 12 stars. The number 12 is considered a mark of excellence.

title: TIUE students at the photo exhibition "We are the children" - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE students at the photo exhibition "We are the children"
content:
On 18-19 November, students of Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) visited the photo exhibition “We are the children”, which takes place in the Humo Arena and is organized by the Ministry of Preschool Education of the Republic of Uzbekistan and UNESCO.

The photo exhibition “We are the children” aims to draw attention to the problems of growing up and raising preschool children. The works of photographers from Asia, Africa, North and South America were presented. It is important to note that the photographs also attracted attention to the problems of inclusivity in the process of education and training.

An important symbol of the exhibition was a luminous spreading tree that took root in the very center of the exhibition, in the image of which is the entire education system that accompanies the formation of personality from the earliest years. The students showed great interest in each exposition of the exhibition, were interested in its meaning and purpose.

TIUE management expresses gratitude to the organizers of the photo exhibition “We are the children”.

title: The closing ceremony of Huawei's educational project "Seeds for the Future 2022" was held in Tashkent - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: The closing ceremony of Huawei's educational project "Seeds for the Future 2022" was held in Tashkent
content:
The closing ceremony of Huawei’s educational project “Seeds for the Future 2022” was held in Tashkent

Students of seven universities of Uzbekistan took part in Huawei’s annual training program “Seeds for the Future 2022”, aimed at studying advanced technologies and the impact of digital solutions on the sustainable development of the world.

During the project, participants took courses in such IT areas as 5G, Artificial Intelligence, Cloud Computing, Digital Energy, Huawei mobile services, and also improved their leadership skills and teamwork skills, studied Chinese culture and shared the traditions of Uzbekistan with participants from Austria, Rwanda and Serbia.

From 14 to 21 November, students from Tashkent University of Information Technology (TUIT), Amiti University in Tashkent, Inha University in Tashkent, Kime International University in Tashkent, Turin Polytechnic University, Tashkent International University of Education and the Tashkent Institute of Irrigation and Agricultural Mechanization Engineers took part in Huawei’s flagship educational project “Seeds for the Future”.

On 23 November, the closing ceremony of “Seeds for the Future 2022” took place, which for the eighth time brought together university students of Uzbekistan to improve knowledge and skills in the IT field. Students of seven educational institutions of Uzbekistan have completed a program for in-depth study of IT technologies along with participants from Austria, Rwanda and Serbia. Lectures, master classes, and open discussions with Huawei’s international experts, as well as experts from partner organizations, were organized over the course of eight days. The project was held in a hybrid format at Huawei’s office in Tashkent with direct inclusions from headquarters and virtual tours of technological showrooms, as well as with the online participation of mentors on leadership development, startup launch and business ideas implementation.

The main task within the framework of participation in the program was the development of its Tech4Good business project using digital solutions to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (UN SDGs). Students of Uzbekistan presented their works in such areas as eliminating hunger and ensuring a healthy lifestyle, combating climate change and its consequences, rational use of water resources and environmental protection. The development of Tech4Good projects within the framework of the Seeds for the Future program has been implemented since last year to attract the younger generation to the problems of sustainable development of the world and finding solutions to achieve the SDGs as soon as possible through the introduction of information technologies. After completing the training, the teams have the opportunity to finalize their projects according to the comments and advice received, and take part in the Tech4Good global competition, which will be held in January 2023.

Today, after the teams presented their Tech4Good projects, we see that the world of digital technologies is open to absolutely everyone and everyone can contribute to the sustainable development of the world. The courses presented for the participants of 2022 included the study of advanced technologies such as 5G, AI, Cloud computing, as well as the theme of green development based on digital energy and the introduction of renewable energy sources, which is currently an urgent agenda of many states. I hope that the knowledge acquired within the framework of the program will help students in their subsequent professional growth,” said Mr. Chen Jiakai, Director of Huawei in Uzbekistan.

“Seeds for the Future” is a global program of Huawei, implemented within the framework of corporate social responsibility. In previous years, 50 students of Uzbekistan took courses under the program in China, and 54 students took part in the online version of the program in 2020 – 2021. In 2017, 4 participants of the Seeds for the Future project from Uzbekistan took part in the One Belt, One Road International Summit in Beijing.

title: TIUE students visit a multimedia exhibition dedicated to the works of Van Gogh - Tashkent International University of Education

During the visit to the exhibition, students got acquainted with the main milestones of Van Gogh’s life and creative path, as well as directly with a number of his works presented in multimedia format. Special attention is drawn to the atmosphere in which the exhibition takes place, as it is prepared in a modern style and promotes immersion in the thoughts of the artist himself.

The students received positive impressions from visiting the exhibition and at the same time paid attention not only to the artist’s work, but also to his way of thinking and personality. It is important to note that master classes on the works of Van Gogh are also held on the territory of the exhibition.

The administration of the Tashkent International University of Education expresses its gratitude to the organizers of the exhibition.

content: The aim objective of MMIT’24 is to provide a world class platform to present and discuss all the latest research and results of scientists related to Innovations in Science, Modern Education and teaching technology, ICT in Education, Digital learning and learning management and other related fields. This conference provides opportunities for the different areas delegates to exchange new ideas and application experiences face to face, to establish business or research relations and to find global partners for future collaboration. title: Teaching and learning strategy of Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Teaching and learning strategy of Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE)
content:
Tashkent International University of Education is actively working to develop a teaching and learning strategy with the informational support of the British Council and Advance HE as part of the Change Academy project, which is carried out in Uzbekistan in cooperation with the Ministry of Higher and Secondary Specialized Education of the Republic of Uzbekistan (MHSSE). The strategy is being prepared for publication by the end of December 2022.

The goal of this strategy is to internationalize the educational processes of the University and prepare highly qualified specialists who will be able to think innovatively and create jobs, have flexible and soft skills in accordance with their chosen specialization.

As part of a project aimed at improving the quality of education in higher education institutions in the 2020-2021 academic year, the Tashkent International University of Education was included in the 12 universities of Uzbekistan selected to participate in the Change Academy program.

Participants of the project, which included the Vice-rector for the International cooperation and Projects development as the team leader (Victoria Babakhodjaeva), the First Vice-rector for educational issues (Oybek Sheraliev), the Dean (Gulrukh Abdusalamova), Head of the academic department (Shavkat Djabbarov), Head of the Business and Information Technology Faculty (Ruslan Sultanov), Head of the Student Services Department (Guzalia Nugmankhodjaeva), as well as the President of the TIUE Student’s Union represented by Umid Ravshanov, and a member of the TIUE Student’s Union Amal Fayzullaev for 6 months participated in the online seminars and trainings.

The program aimed at strengthening cooperation with leading institutions in the field of higher education and quality assurance agencies, in support of the reform of the national higher education system in the areas of continuous quality assurance of teaching, internationalization of higher education, ensuring the relevance of education to the requirements of the modern labor market, clearly defining expected learning outcomes graduates for successful employment.

Also, the program aimed to help university representatives acquire leadership and management skills for the transition to organizational, financial and academic sustainability, as well as help improve the quality of teaching and learning through initiative and trust management.

title: Information about Education and Training Management - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Information about Education and Training Management
content:
Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) offers to prospective students the three-year program “Management of education and educational processes”, which is the first such program in Uzbekistan.

The purpose of program is to train professionals in the field of management in secondary special and higher education with managerial, pedagogical, as well as methodological knowledge and skills

When studying this program, TIUE aims to develop the competencies and skills in developing educational strategies necessary for the implementation of effective educational programs, planning educational processes, administering educational systems, taking into account the need for the development of inclusive and STEAM education.

The strongest teaching staff, consisting of international and local experts, pays special attention to practical classes – workshops, field trips, master-classes – where intensive interaction of all participants in the educational process is ensured, the transfer of practical experience from the teacher to the students.

Since education is one of the dynamically developing business areas that can bring high and stable income, graduates will gain the knowledge necessary to work as a highly qualified specialists both in the education sector, in international and domestic education-related companies, in various educational organizations (institutions of higher education, schools, centers of additional education, etc.), and in state structures.

title: Higher education in Uzbekistan: what you need to know
meta_description: Let's figure out what to choose: public or private higher education in Tashkent. We will consider all the advantages, features and prospects for graduates. Find out the pros and cons of higher education institutions in Uzbekistan to make the right decision!
content:
The higher education system in Uzbekistan plays an important role in training professionals who are ready to work effectively in various sectors of the national economy and scientific activities. The republic’s educational structure includes three categories of higher education institutions: state universities, branches of foreign universities, and private sector universities. Each of these categories has its own specific characteristics and features of operation.

The higher education system in the Republic of Uzbekistan has many features that are important to consider when choosing a university. The country has a wide range of state-funded higher education institutions, as well as branches of foreign universities and private universities. Therefore, applicants have great prospects in terms of choosing the higher education that suits them best.

- State funding — most higher education institutions receive support from the state, which affects the cost of tuition and the quality of educational programmes.

- A wide range of educational programmes — state institutions, branches of foreign universities and private universities offer a variety of specialisations, allowing students to find the right programme for them.

- Mandatory state licensing and accreditation — all educational institutions must comply with state-established quality standards.

- A high level of control by state authorities helps to maintain a stable level of education and programmes implemented by each university in the Republic of Uzbekistan.

- The availability of international programmes — both public and private universities are offering internationally accredited educational programmes, which expands opportunities for students to study abroad.

- Development of material and technical resources — in recent years, Uzbekistan has been actively investing in public and private educational institutions, creating comfortable conditions for study and scientific work.

These features form the basis of the education system in the Republic of Uzbekistan, making it modern and compliant with international standards. They enable the creation of optimal conditions for applicants and students in every educational institution, both public and private.

The choice between a state, foreign or private university depends on many factors: the student’s goals, financial capabilities and educational preferences. Education in the Republic of Uzbekistan continues to develop, and all universities are striving to improve the quality of the educational process. Ultimately, it is not only the name of the institution that is important, but also the knowledge, skills and experience gained, which will help graduates to successfully realise their potential in their professional field.

title: Beeline Academy opens at TIUE - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Beeline Academy opens at TIUE
content:
On 3 October, the Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) hosted the opening ceremony of the Beeline Academy, which will help train IT specialists for various sectors of the economy of Uzbekistan.

Beeline Academy will provide young citizens of Uzbekistan with access to both online learning and a distance learning platform. Particular attention will be paid to the training of cybersecurity specialists who will ensure reliable protection of the data of private and government organizations from potential threats.

At the first stage, Beeline Uzbekistan invested US$150,000 in a project whose goal is to promote IT professions and attract youth of Uzbekistan to industries directly involved in the digitalization of the country’s economy. Cooperation with the university opens up new horizons and makes it possible to create a system platform for training IT specialists.

TIUE is one of the private universities in Uzbekistan, which trains highly qualified specialists with higher education for the needs of the real sector of the economy of Uzbekistan in the field of information technology, education and business management. In partnership with Beeline Academy, it is also planned to train teachers to conduct courses and trainings for young talents throughout the country.

“The education sector does not always keep up with the needs of the labor market and the emergence of new technologies. Therefore, the participation of large businesses with experience in implementing digital projects and extensive expertise in the IT field is very important. We are grateful to Beeline Uzbekistan for creating such an ambitious project on the basis of our university. I am confident that Beeline Academy graduates will join the ranks of the most successful and sought-after professionals in the field of information technology,” shared Nosir Khojaev, rector of TIUE.

“Quality education of young people and the creation of a corporate culture in the field of digital technologies are important tasks of the company in the process of transformation into a digital operator. Our goal is to become a leader in EduTech as well. The launch of Beeline Academy is a key step in this direction. The new educational platform will provide training opportunities for young talent, allowing them to acquire advanced knowledge and skills in the field of IT, cybersecurity and artificial intelligence. We will also actively search for and identify the most gifted students among students for further cooperation,” said Andrzej Malinowski, CEO of Beeline Uzbekistan.

An important aspect of the Beeline Academy project, in addition to preparing and equipping classrooms, is the creation of its own interactive video studio, which will become an integral part of the educational process. Within the studio, students and teachers will have access to modern technologies, which will help in improving distance learning, developing virtual video lessons, conducting webinars and video conferencing, as well as creating high-quality video content in order to make the educational process more accessible and interactive.

On the opening day, Kaan Terzioglu, CEO of VEON Group, which includes Beeline Uzbekistan, addressed Beeline Academy students live.

“From the very beginning, when we talked about our ambitions in Uzbekistan, we had one main goal – to make Uzbekistan an IT center in this region. Developing talent in this area will help the country integrate into the global digital economy. I am proud to be part of this process. Your task in the future will be to process data, create software, and effectively implement artificial intelligence. I wish you a fantastic experience and want Beeline Academy to become part of the legacy that we are creating in Uzbekistan,” said VEON Group CEO Kaan Terzioglu.

The Beeline Academy project will increase students’ access to modern, hands-on training programs along with lessons within the university’s information technology curriculum.

title: ORIENTATION DAY takes place at TIUE - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: ORIENTATION DAY takes place at TIUE
content:
On 3 October 2023, Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) organized the ORIENTATION DAY event in connection with the start of the 2023-2024 academic year.

The event was attended by first-year students and their parents, as well as the university administration and faculty members.

The event was opened by the rector of the university Nasir Khojaev. In his speech, the rector informed students about the conditions created at the university for students, the educational process, internal procedures and rules of conduct for students, the organization of exams.

At the end of the event, training was organized to introduce students to each other and form friendly relations between them. Students also took part in excursions around the building and territory of the university.

Also on the same day, the opening ceremony of the Beeline Academy, created at the university in accordance with a memorandum of cooperation between Beeline Uzbekistan and Tashkent International University of Education, took place. Andrzej Malinowski, General Director of Beeline Uzbekistan, attended in it.

The Beeline Academy, created on the basis of the university, plans to attract young people to the field directly related to the digitalization of the economy of Uzbekistan and contribute to the training of IT specialists for various sectors of the economy.

title: Students of TIUE pay visit to Tashkent school - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Students of TIUE pay visit to Tashkent school
content:
As part of the program for organizing practical work, the Tashkent International University of Education organized a visit of the freshmen students in the direction of “Management of education and educational processes” to school No. 110 in Mirabad district of Tashkent.

Students attended the lessons of highly qualified teachers in different classes of primary and secondary schools. During the lessons, teachers shared their experience in using pedagogical methods and innovative technologies, the widespread use of which was facilitated by the excellent technical equipment of the school. The students were very impressed and inspired by watching the process of organizing and conducting the lessons. Everyone got attention.

All students were assigned teachers who answered all the questions of interest to students. This excursion helped students to realize even more deeply the most important role of a modern teacher in our time.

TIUE expresses special gratitude to the leadership of school No. 110 for the high-quality organization of demonstration classes, professional management and hospitality!

title: Tashkent International University of Education announces entrance exam dates - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Tashkent International University of Education announces entrance exam dates
content:
Tashkent International University of Education is excited to announce the dates for our entrance exams for prospective students for 2023-2024 academic year. The exams will be held on 26 and 27 May, 16 and 30 June, 14 and 30 July, as well as 4, 11, 18 and 25 August.

Prospective students will be tested in mathematics and language, with the language exam depending on the language of instruction (Uzbek, Russian or English). Those who successfully pass the exams will be admitted to the university.

“We’re thrilled to welcome the next generation of students to Tashkent International University of Education,” said the university’s rector Nosir Khodjaev. “Our entrance exams are an opportunity for students to showcase their knowledge and skills, and we can’t wait to see what they bring to the table.”

In addition to the scheduled entrance exam dates, the university is open to holding additional exams in September for students who may have missed the previous exams.

“At Tashkent International University of Education, we’re committed to providing a world-class education that prepares our students for success in their future careers. Our expert faculty, cutting-edge facilities, and vibrant community of students make our university the perfect place to start your academic journey,” the rector underlined.

For more information on our entrance exams and admissions process, please visit our website or contact our admissions office.

Imam Bukhariy street, Tuzel-2 block, Yashnabad district, Tashkent city

The university is a 15-minute walk from the metro station “3-Bekat” and behind the school number 255.

title: TIUE conducts the first entrance exams for the 2023-2024 academic year - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE conducts the first entrance exams for the 2023-2024 academic year
content:
On 26 May 2023, Tashkent International University of Education held the first entrance exams for applicants for the 2023-2024 academic year.

These exams included sections in mathematics and language (Uzbek, Russian or English depending on the language of instruction). These exams are an important step in the process of admission to the university.

TIUE is committed to attracting the most talented and motivated students who are committed to developing their skills and excel in their chosen fields. Entrance examinations are a significant stage that allows the university to assess the knowledge and abilities of applicants, as well as to make sure that they are prepared for the educational process.

All examinations were conducted in strict accordance with established norms and requirements, ensuring honesty and fairness in the student selection process. The TIUE team carefully organized the exams to create all the necessary conditions for applicants.

The next stage of the admission process is the evaluation of examination results and the decision to admit students. Exam results will be announced within 20 days.

It should be noted that Tashkent International University of Education will also hold another entrance exams on 27 May.

The university plans to conduct entrance examinations monthly till September. The next exams are scheduled for 16 and 30 June, 14 and 30 July, and 4, 11, 18 and 25 August. The university also may have additional exams for applicants in September. Applicants who failed to pass the exams can retake the entrance exams.

In order to participate in the entrance exams, applicants must register and submit necessary application on the website https://apply.tiue.uz.

title: TIUE Rector takes part in the III International Legal Forum “Tashkent Law Spring” - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE Rector takes part in the III International Legal Forum “Tashkent Law Spring”
content:
Rector of Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) Nosir Khodjaev took part in the III International Legal Forum “Tashkent Law Spring”.

Tashkent Law Spring is one of the significant events in the field of law in the Central Asian region. The forum is held once every two years under the auspices of the Ministry of Justice of Uzbekistan.

According to the organizers, the main topic of this forum is “Fundamental Rights as Eternal Values”. The Tashkent Law Spring has become an open platform for discussion by international, foreign and domestic experts of modern legal trends through the prism of fundamental rights.

TIUE Rector Nosir Khodjaev took part in the session “Digital Technologies in the Legal Sector” and made a presentation “Digitalization and Improving the Legal Competence of Higher Education Students”.

“Developing the legal literacy of students from all areas of universities using modern digital technologies not only prepares them for a successful career in their chosen field, but also develops their critical thinking skills, problem-solving and teamwork,” said the head of the university.

“In the era of digitalization and the information society, understanding legal principles and skills is becoming increasingly important for every person, regardless of their professional field,” Nosir Khodjaev added.

He noted that digital technologies provide unique opportunities to enrich the educational process and expand access to legal information.

The TIUE rector also spoke about technologies – virtual and augmented reality, electronic resources and platforms, and artificial intelligence that can be used in the educational process.

title: The scientific-practical conference on modern methods and innovative technologies in education takes place at TIUE - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: The scientific-practical conference on modern methods and innovative technologies in education takes place at TIUE
content:
Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) hosted a scientific-practical conference on “Modern methods and innovation technologies in education: Present status, important questions and challenges” on 25 May.

The conference brought together students, experts, practitioners, and researchers in the field of education who shared their scientific achievements, experience, and best practices. The conference provided participants with a platform for exchanging experiences and discussing key trends and challenges faced by modern educational institutions.

Participants had the opportunity to familiarize themselves with the latest methods and innovative approaches used in education, as well as to discuss and identify current issues and challenges faced by modern educational institutions. The conference consisted of three sections covering various aspects of modern education.

During the section on “Modern Education and Pedagogy: Trends, Issues, and Development Prospects,” participants discussed relevant issues related to the development of modern pedagogical practices and analyzed the prospects for the development of the educational system.

In the section on “Modern Information and Communication Technologies in Education,” participants discussed and analyzed the role and impact of modern information and communication technologies on the teaching and learning process and the development of the educational environment.

The section on “Digital Learning or Digital University as a New Approach to Education Organization and Management” explored the latest approaches to digital learning and examined the role of the digital university in modern education organization and management.

Speakers at the conference were awarded certificates by Tashkent International University of Education.

title: TIUE hosts a workshop dedicated to freelance - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE hosts a workshop dedicated to freelance
content:
On 16 May, Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) held a seminar “Freelance is the profession of the future”, organized jointly with the Ministry of Digital Technologies, the Center for Digital Economy Research and freelance.uz.

The event attracted over 150 young people interested in using their skills to make money. As part of the seminar, invited specialists and experts in the field of freelance shared their experience and knowledge with the participants.

The seminar was opened by TIUE Rector Nosir Khodjaev, who noted the active development of the freelance market in Uzbekistan, which provides young people with language and computer skills with the opportunity to find work and earn money.

Among the speakers at the event were invited experts: Sardor Islamov, GDE expert, Ibrahim Isroilov, data analyst, and Kamron Juraev, front-end programmer. They shared their experience of working on various foreign freelance platforms.

The seminar “Freelance is the profession of the future” has become a valuable source of information and inspiration for all participants. Participants had the opportunity to get acquainted with the possibilities of freelancing and learn how to apply their skills to achieve financial independence and career development.

title: The Head of the Department at TIUE successfully defends PhD dissertation - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: The Head of the Department at TIUE successfully defends PhD dissertation
content:
Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) is proud to announce that the Head of the Department of Education and Training Management of TIUE Rauf Bekbayev has successfully defended his dissertation for the degree of Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in the Scientific Council of the National University of Uzbekistan.

The dissertation titled “René Guénon and His Place in the Synthesis of Traditionalism and Intercultural Dialogue (a Comparative Analysis)” represents a significant contribution to the field of philosophy and the history of philosophy. The work is conducted in the specialty 09.00.03 – History of Philosophy and is dedicated to the theoretical and methodological aspects of philosophical comparativism, intercultural philosophy, and traditionalism philosophy.

This dissertation holds academic importance and is of interest to a wide range of researchers and scholars in the field of social and humanitarian sciences, including political science, cultural studies, and pedagogy. Rauf Bekbayev’s work contributes to a deeper understanding of the essence of philosophical concepts and their influence on international education and cultural dialogue.

The team of Tashkent International University of Education congratulates Rauf Bekbayev on this significant achievement and expresses confidence in his future success in the field of academic research.

title: Graduates of TIUE may have the opportunity to work in Japanese companies - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Graduates of TIUE may have the opportunity to work in Japanese companies
content:
Graduates of the Information Technology Department at Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) may have the opportunity for employment in Japan.

Tashkent International University of Education has signed an agreement with CAL Co. Ltd (Japan) зт the development and utilization of human resources for employment in Japan.

The agreement was signed by the Rector of TIUE, Nosir Khodjaev, and the CEO of CAL Co. Ltd, Akira Maeda, on 27 July 2023, in the city of Tashkent.

The main purpose of this agreement is to prepare highly qualified specialists in the field of information technology for companies in Japan. In accordance with the agreement, TIUE will be engaged in training specialists who possess the necessary knowledge and skills in the field of information technology, as well as proficiency in the Japanese language, enabling graduates to successfully integrate into the Japanese labor market.

CAL Co. Ltd, in turn, will conduct the selection and testing of candidates, provide consultations on employment matters in Japan, and create favorable conditions for the successful adaptation of specialists from Uzbekistan in Japan.

This cooperation opens up new prospects for the exchange of knowledge and experience between TIUE and CAL Co. Ltd, contributing to the strengthening of friendly relations between Uzbekistan and Japan, as well as promoting education and innovation in the field of information technology and the employment of Uzbekistan’s youth.

title: TIUE to hold entrance exams everyday - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE to hold entrance exams everyday
content:
Tashkent International University of Education, in order to provide maximum convenience and accessibility for all applicants, is starting to conduct entrance exams on a daily basis (from Monday to Friday).

Applicants must fill out an online application on the apply.tiue.uz website and after the application is approved, applicants must visit the TIUE campus at a convenient time from Monday to Friday from 9:30 to 15:00 to take exams.

Exams for all departments are held in mathematics and the chosen language of instruction (Uzbek, Russian or English). Exams are FREE!

This innovation gives applicants greater flexibility and the ability to choose a convenient date and time for taking exams. Now they can easily tailor the admissions process to fit their schedule and preferences.

Admission campaign for Information Technology, Business Management, Education and Training Management, as well as in the International Foundation Program will last until 31 August 2023.

title: TIUE reduces tuition fees for correspondence students - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE reduces tuition fees for correspondence students
content:
Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) is pleased to announce a reduction in the tuition fees for all correspondence programs starting from the 2023-2024 academic year.

The tuition fee for correspondence programs at TIUE has been reduced from 18 million soums to 14 million soums for one academic year, commencing from the 2023-2024 academic year.

It should be noted that the reduction in tuition fees for students in the correspondence departments does not apply retroactively; it only affects the upcoming academic year.

Applicants can apply to one of two correspondence programs at TIUE – Information Technology and Business Management. The admission campaign will end on 31 August 2023. Documents can be submitted online through the apply.tiue.uz website. For transfers, documents can be submitted through the transfer.tiue.uz website.

The languages of instruction are Uzbek and Russian.

The entrance exam consists of 2 subjects: mathematics and Uzbek or Russian language. Moreover, EXAMS ARE FREE!

title: Rector of TIUE take part in the Digital Leadership Uzbekistan 2023 forum - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Rector of TIUE take part in the Digital Leadership Uzbekistan 2023 forum
content:
On 7 July 2023, the Rector of Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE), Nosir Khodjaev, participated in the international forum Digital Leadership Uzbekistan 2023, held in Tashkent.

The Digital Leadership Uzbekistan 2023 forum is a unique opportunity to learn about the rapidly evolving and relevant advancements in the field of IT education.

In particular, Nosir Khodjaev attended the EdTech Session event, dedicated to the development of IT education and featuring leaders from prestigious international organizations and universities.

This event was aimed to explore innovative teaching methods, enhance the potential of IT education in Uzbekistan, exchange experiences and knowledge among experts from leading countries, cultivate youth in accordance with future technological skills, and discuss current issues in the education system.

During the forum, international experts from the United States, Germany, India, Bangladesh, South Korea, and the People’s Republic of China shared their experiences in presentations and panel discussions.

It is worth mentioning that Tashkent International University of Education has a faculty of Information Technology. The languages of instruction are Uzbek, English, and Russian.

title: TIUE student to take part in Seeds for the Future 2023 in Doha - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE student to take part in Seeds for the Future 2023 in Doha
content:
Tashkent International University of Education is pleased to announce that student Kamila Nazarova has successfully become a participant in the Seeds for the Future 2023 project from Huawei.

The Seeds for the Future program offers students a unique opportunity to gain knowledge of modern information and communication technologies, as well as immerse themselves in the culture and technical experience of one of the world’s leading technology companies.

Kamila Nazarova has been selected to participate in this prestigious project, which provides students with a unique opportunity to immerse themselves in the world of advanced information and communication technologies.

Within the framework of this project, students representing different countries will take part in specialized events aimed at developing IT education and improving their knowledge and skills in the field of technology.

This year’s Seeds for the Future 2023 Regional Stage will take place from 2 to 9 September in Doha, Qatar.

Based on the results of participation at this stage, Kamila Nazarova may have the opportunity to undergo additional training, which will be held in Dubai (UAE) from 10 to 16 September 2023. Additional training will provide her with additional opportunities for professional development and broadening her horizons in the field of technology.

TIUE is proud of the talents of its students and sincerely congratulates Kamila Nazarova on this significant achievement. We believe this is an important step in her educational and professional growth, and well-deserved recognition of her potential and diligence.

We wish Kamila Nazarova successful and productive participation in the Seeds for the Future 2023 project, as well as successful representation of our university on this international platform. We are confident that she will prove to be a worthy representative of TIUE and bring honor to our university and country.

title: Professors and teachers of TIUE are preparing for the new academic year - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Professors and teachers of TIUE are preparing for the new academic year
content:

The training was developed by Gerson Lapid, a highly qualified foreign lecturer at the University, who infused his training materials with the knowledge and skills he gained from the problem-solving programs of the education system at the Republic Polytechnic University of Singapore.

title: Happy holidays, Dear Teachers! - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Happy holidays, Dear Teachers!
content:
The role of a mentor is very important in the development of each person’s personality, as well as in his formation as a professional.

In addition to knowledge, selfless, faithful teachers instill the noblest qualities in their students, filling their hearts with kindness and humanity. The successes of our sons and daughters, who achieve high results with their talents, abilities and deep knowledge in various fields, are primarily the result of the hard work of highly respected teachers. The announcement of October 1 as the Day of Teachers and Mentors in our country is the highest recognition of the work of many thousands of teachers, mentors and teachers who make an outstanding contribution to the development of Uzbekistan’s independence.

The renewed Uzbekistan is successfully carrying out reforms and the formation of our country on a par with the most developed and modern countries of the world will undoubtedly be achieved through the development of science and education.

Thanks to the sensitive attention of the state to educational institutions, new opportunities are being created to strengthen the material and technical base, improve the skills of teachers and mentors, strengthen scientific and pedagogical cooperation with leading educational centers abroad, introduce modern educational and information technologies, as well as new teaching methods in the educational process.

On behalf of the administration and the staff of Tashkent International University of Education, we congratulate you on October 1 — the Day of Teachers and Mentors, we wish you good health, happiness and good luck, peace and prosperity to your home!
