title: TIUE and AKFA university become partners - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE and AKFA university become partners
content:
TIUE has successfully made AKFA university in Uzbekistan its reliable partner

On the 5th of March the Rector of TIUE Ulugbek Kasimkhodjaev and the Rector of AKFA university Bekhzod Djalilov have signed Memorandum about mutual understanding.

Both universities reached the deal of accepting the degrees of students graduated from educational courses (foundation), bachelor and postgraduates.

Now, enrollees at TIUE, who successfully completed foundation course are able to continue their further education at AKFA university beginning from 2nd course without any entrance examination for following majors:

* Accounting and Finance (by degree of IFP and major of Business and IT)

* Economics (by degree of IFP, major of Business and after certificate about completing preparation courses in Math from Transformation Hub, TIUE)

Language course (by degree of IFP, major of Business)

Program engineering (by degree of IFP, major IT and certificate about completing certificate about completing preparation courses in Math from Transformation Hub, TIUE)

title: Visits to academic lyceums in Tashkent - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Visits to academic lyceums in Tashkent
content:
A delegation of TIUE paid a visit to academic lyceums in Tashkent.

Academic director of TIUE Islam El Xadari told about current program of International Foundation, which is taught by 2 directions: Business and IT.

During the presentation, the students were informed that they can take evening courses of spring intake and get international diploma by the end of August. The document gives an opportunity to get an entrance to prestigious universities of the world in September. Consequently, the students will graduate from the university one year earlier.

70% of teachers at TIUE are foreign experts, therefore lessons are taught in English. So in order to get an entrance to TIUE students must have at least basic knowledge of English language, equivalent IELTS score 4.5

In the end of the presentation the students were interested in features of TIUE

title: New Partnership with Monash University - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: New Partnership with Monash University
content:
International educational lanes can be counted as the most important and perspective majors of International cultural exchange.

TIUE is dynamically expanding cooperation with world-ranked universities and one of them is the Australian government university with various branches, which is on the 55th position among the world universities in QS World University Rankings 2021 – Monash University.

Signed Memorandum is about mutual understanding between both universities and directed to scientific cooperation and creation of cooperative, educational and language programs, exchanging academic materials, available for both sides, cooperative seminars, master classes and other academic events.

TIUE is not limited with only local partners, it is continuously working on the partnership with leading universities in the world.

title: TIUE and KFU to collaborate in the field of education - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE and KFU to collaborate in the field of education
content:
Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) has established cooperation with Kazan (Volga) Federal University (KFU, Russia) to develop educational and scientific programs.

The signed memorandum of understanding between the Rector of Tashkent International University of Education Nosir Khodjaev and the Director of the Elabuga Institute of Kazan (Volga) Federal University, Elena Merzon, aims to foster international cooperation and strengthen ties between the two educational institutions in the field of education and science.

This is in line with the Intergovernmental Agreement between the Republic of Uzbekistan and the Russian Federation on Cooperation in Higher Education, signed on 27 July 1995.

Within the framework of the memorandum, joint educational and scientific programs in the field of education will be developed and implemented. Tashkent International University of Education and Kazan (Volga) Federal University will facilitate student exchanges, as well as exchanges of educators and researchers for the purposes of learning, professional development, and the advancement of scientific and educational activities, including international academic exchange.

Additionally, both parties plan to conduct joint scientific research, fundamental and applied research in the field of education, and engage in collaborative innovation activities. They also intend to organize international educational, research, and technical projects, congresses, symposiums, conferences, seminars, and exchange educational and scientific literature.

“We are delighted to establish cooperation with Kazan (Volga) Federal University as it opens up new prospects for the development of our university and the exchange of knowledge and experience between the two countries. We are confident that joint educational and scientific programs will lead to improvements in the quality of education and scientific achievements in our institutions and contribute to the overall development of science and education,” noted Rector Nosir Khodjaev of TIUE.

Kazan Federal University ranks 322nd in international rankings and is one of the leading universities in the fields of education and pedagogy.

title: TIUE students benefit from a master class on resume building by an English language educator - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE students benefit from a master class on resume building by an English language educator
content:
Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) recently hosted a master class on resume building for its students, conducted by Hannah Rothwell, an esteemed English Language Educator at the American Corner Namangan. The event was organized by Scarito Alessio, a dedicated teacher at TIUE.

The master class provided students with a unique opportunity to enhance their resume-writing skills and gain valuable insights into crafting an impressive curriculum vitae.

Hannah Rothwell, known for her expertise in English language education, shared her extensive knowledge and experience with the participants. The master class was a condensed version of a three-month workshop that Hannah has developed and curated at the American Corner in Namangan.

The session focused on stimulating creativity and lateral thinking in relation to resume building. Through engaging group activities, students were encouraged to think outside the box and explore innovative ways of presenting their skills and qualifications on a CV. The interactive nature of the master class fostered a dynamic learning environment, allowing students to actively participate and apply the concepts discussed.

Nosir Khodjaev, the Rector of TIUE, expressed his gratitude to Hannah Rothwell for her valuable contribution in conducting the master class. He highlighted the importance of equipping students with essential skills for their future careers and commended the efforts of Scarito Alessio in organizing such enriching educational experiences for the TIUE community.

The master class received positive feedback from the participants, who expressed their appreciation for the practical knowledge gained and the interactive teaching style employed by Hannah Rothwell. Students found the session to be highly informative, empowering them to build stronger resumes and present their qualifications effectively.

The event exemplifies TIUE’s commitment to providing its students with exceptional learning opportunities and preparing them for success in their professional endeavors. The university remains dedicated to collaborating with experienced educators and professionals who can contribute to the holistic development of its students.

title: TIUE hosts a book fair “Build Your Future with Books” - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE hosts a book fair “Build Your Future with Books”
content:
Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) held a book fair “Build your future with books” on 30 May.

Several vendors presented a wide selection of books to students and faculty of the university. One of the guests at the event was the Uzbek writer Safar Olloyor, who presented his new book.

The book fair “Build Your Future with Books” became a platform for the university community, where everyone could expand their horizons and enrich themselves with knowledge.

Various book companies offered a varied selection of books covering a wide range of topics from non-fiction to fiction.

One of the highlights of the fair was the presence of the famous Uzbek writer Safar Olloyor. He presented his latest book and talked about his life and work.

Build Your Future with Books Book Fair reflects the importance of reading and education in the lives of TIUE students and faculty.

title: TIUE and IT Investments Center are joining forces to help young people to make money through international freelance platforms - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE and IT Investments Center are joining forces to help young people to make money through international freelance platforms
content:
Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) and IT Investments Center will collaborate to develop information technology and provide educational opportunities for youth to earn on international freelance platforms.

The partnership was formalized through the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between Nosir Khodjaev, the Rector of Tashkent International University of Education, and Abduali Khaidarov, the CEO of IT Investments Center.

According to the provisions of this document, the parties intend to utilize the potential of information technology to equip students with the necessary skills for freelance work and create conditions for their earnings. This initiative is aimed not only at strengthening the position of youth but also at improving their employment prospects, contributing to the development of IT service exports.

TIUE and IT Investments Center will attract freelancers to train students. The goal of both parties is to provide students with the relevant knowledge and skills required for successful work in the digital economy.

The emphasis will be on practical training, granting students access to modern technologies and innovative platforms that will enable them to master contemporary tools and methods for working on international freelance platforms.

This partnership opens up new opportunities for the youth of Uzbekistan, providing them with the opportunity for education and earning in the field of information technology. The youth will be able to develop their skills, gain practical experience, and apply them in the global freelance service markets. Such collaboration contributes to the strengthening of the digital economy and enhances the competitiveness of Uzbekistan in the global IT industry.

title: Tashkent International University of Education starts accepting applications for transfer students - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Tashkent International University of Education starts accepting applications for transfer students
content:
Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) is pleased to announce the start of accepting transfer applications for students from local and foreign higher education institutions. This provides a unique opportunity for students to continue their education at TIUE, which offers a variety of programs.

Transfer applications are accepted online through the website https://transfer.tiue.uz/login/index.php until 31 August 2023.

TIUE encourages all interested students to submit their applications by the deadline and take advantage of this opportunity to further develop their academic and professional skills.

To transfer training to TIUE, you must provide an academic transcript (transcript) for all disciplines passed and a passport or ID card.

If there is a difference between the subjects indicated in the academic transcript (transcript) and the TIUE subjects, not exceeding 60 TIUE credits or three subjects, the transfer of studies can only be carried out in the corresponding direction (close to the direction of study).

TIUE also provides an opportunity for students who have a difference of up to 60 TIUE credits or three subjects listed on the academic transcript to retake the different subjects at TIUE.

If there is a difference in the amount of more than 60 TIUE credits or three subjects, the University has the right to consider each case on a separate basis.

title: Gifts were handed over to children within International Children’s Day - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Gifts were handed over to children within International Children’s Day
content:
On the eve of International Children’s Day, which is celebrated on 1 June, students and staff of Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) organized a special event aimed at supporting children in need of social protection.

The event, held on the territory of the TIUE University, was filled with joy, fun and care for children.

Students and staff of the university prepared memorable gifts for children who need social support. The gifts included toys, books, stationery, and other useful items.

This event was a manifestation of the care and support on the part of TIUE students and staff towards children in need of special help and support. All project participants expressed their sincere joy and gratitude for the opportunity to bring smiles to children on such a special day.

TIUE Rector Nosir Khodjaev expressed pride and recognized the importance of such events that help create a positive environment and contribute to the social responsibility and development of the university. He expressed his gratitude to all the students and staff who took the initiative and contributed to the organization of this wonderful event.

“We believe in the importance of cultivating civic values and conscious responsibility to society. We are proud of our students and staff who care about others and are actively involved in such social initiatives. We will continue to support and inspire our educational community to make positive changes in our society and make the world a better place for all children,” said the rector of the university.

TIUE strives not only to provide quality education but also to contribute to the social development and well-being of society. The organization of such events is part of the university’s strategy to create an educational environment in which students develop their skills, not only academically but also socially.

title: TIUE students hold master class - Tashkent International University of Education

The master class was held by students Jasmin Yusupjanova and Orif Makhmudkhodjaev on the topic “Schools of the Future” within the framework of the module “The Role of the Teacher in the Modern School”.

The seminar was aimed both at developing the pedagogical skills of students and at forming the relationship between freshmen and sophomore students.

The method of a binary lecture was used, during which future managers in the field of education acquainted freshmen students with the features of introducing innovations and the latest technologies in modern schools, which corresponds to current trends in the development of the national education system.

The main purpose of the visit is to get acquainted with the activities of the organization to encourage young professionals to independently determine their career, personal and professional development and work in the field of information technology.

Employees of IT Park acquainted students with the activities of the organization, current work, projects and programs.

In addition, students were informed about available training courses at information technology centers and internship opportunities at resident companies. It is worth noting that such internships allow students to understand in which area they will work in the future.

title: Representatives of TIUE and Dongshin University hold talks - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Representatives of TIUE and Dongshin University hold talks
content:
On 21 April 2022, a meeting was held between representatives of Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) and Dongshin University.

During the meeting, Seongrok Oh from Dongshin University and Nosir Khodjaev, the rector of TIUE, provided information about the two universities.

Seongrok Oh, representative of Dongshin University, said he hoped that cooperation between the two countries would strengthen ties between the two countries and they discussed the possibility of opening a Korean center at TIUE, improving the qualifications of the faculty, establishing student exchange programs, and developing joint educational programs.

At the end of the meeting, the two universities discussed joint agreements to train and retrain modern personnel.

Nosir Khodjaev proposed creating a working group of representatives of the two universities to discuss further work. The proposal was supported by Korean partners, and the working group began its work.

The educational practice within the framework of the project “Visits to the production” of our students continues.

title: AIUB and IT Park representatives visit TIUE - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: AIUB and IT Park representatives visit TIUE
content:
On 13 April, the representatives of the American International University of Bangladesh (AIUB) and IT Park visited to the Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE).

The Chairman of the Supervisory Board of the Tashkent International University of Education, Sarvar Babakhodjaev presented information about the educational activities of the TIUE and the conditions created for students and as well as tutors to representatives of AIUB and IT Park.

At the meeting, the cooperation issues between the TIUE and AIUB in the educational process were discussed.

It should be noted that the AIUB is one of the most prestigious educational institutions in the world and mainly provides training in the field of business administration and information technology.

title: TIUE marks the first anniversary of its activities - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE marks the first anniversary of its activities
content:
TIUE held an event to celebrate the first year of the launch of the academic year at the university. The event was attended by students and staff of the university.

During the event, the students with high academic results were awarded with special diplomas.

title: TIUE students at the Virtual Reality Festival - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE students at the Virtual Reality Festival
content:
On 9 December, students of the Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) visited the Virtual Reality Film Festival – Digital Autumn: Sensations as part of the field–trip. The festival was held on the territory of the French Alliance in Tashkent.

The Virtual Reality Film Festival is a platform where VR projects by authors from France, the UK, the USA, South Africa, Argentina and Iraq are presented. These projects have won various awards at international venues. In particular, The Key project was presented at the festival, which received the main award of the Tribeca Film Festival in New York in the interactive Storyscapes category and became the winner of the Venice International Film Festival in 2019.

Students had the opportunity to get acquainted with virtual reality technologies, dive into the virtual world and directly experience the possibilities of modern digital technologies. It is important to note that the French Alliance, on the territory of which the festival was held, is part of the French Alliance Foundation, located in Paris, as well as an official partner of the French Embassy in Tashkent.

title: TIUE students at the International Scientific Conference - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE students at the International Scientific Conference
content:
On 15 December, within the framework of the field-trip, students of the Faculty of Education Management and Educational Processes of the Tashkent International University of Education attended an International Conference for students and young scientists on the topic “Modern trends in the innovative development of science and education in the global world”.

The conference was held at the Hilton Tashkent City Hotel and was organized by Stars International University. The conference was attended by more than 500 participants from various higher educational institutions, research institutions, as well as large companies and organizations, including foreign guests.

During the conference, four panel discussions were organized on various issues of the development of modern science and education in Uzbekistan. Among the participants of the panel discussions were heads of various educational and scientific institutions, large companies, including Rector of the University of Journalism and Mass Communications Sherzodhon Kudrathuzha, Vice-rector of Tashkent State Law University Islombek Rustambekov, Vice-rector of Kokand State Pedagogical Institute Nargiza Babayeva, founder of Certiport InfoTech LLC Gazanfer Ali Mohammad, co-founder and CEO of COO of Acrome Robotics & Riders Efe Subashi and others.

Panel discussions were accompanied by discussions and exchange of opinions. At the end of the conference, the most active participants in the discussions received valuable prizes and awards from the Rector of Stars International University Bahram Kurbanov. The first place for active participation was awarded to the teacher of the department “Management of Education and educational processes” TIUE R.R.Bekbayev, the second place was awarded to the 2nd-year student of the faculty “Management of Education and educational processes” TIUE Zh.Yusupzhanova.

Representatives of our university adequately presented TIUE, thereby attracting the interest of many participants of the conference by the Tashkent International University of Education.

title: TIUE students visited Alfraganus University - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE students visited Alfraganus University
content:
On 16 December, students of the Faculty of “Education Management and Educational Processes” of the Tashkent International University of Education visited Alfraganus University as part of a field-trip and in order to study the educational process at a higher educational institution.

During the field trip, students studied the peculiarities of the organization of the educational process at the university, and also attended classes on the subject of “Philosophy”, which were conducted by university teachers. During the training sessions, modern information and communication technologies and interactive teaching methods were used.

Students showed great interest in the educational process at the university, got the opportunity to develop their educational and professional skills for their future specialty.

title: Master class in TIUE - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Master class in TIUE
content:
On 12 December, a master class on “Ancient culture and philosophy and its place in world history” was organized at the Tashkent International University of Education for students of the direction “Management of education and educational processes”. The master class was conducted by Professor of the National University of Uzbekistan Karimov Rakhmat Rakhmanovich.

Professor R.R.Karimov is one of the leading specialists in the history of philosophy in the republic, who stands at the foundation of the formation of a research school of philosophical comparativism and intercultural philosophy in our country. He is the author of textbooks and teaching aids, the supervisor of a number of Doctors of Philosophy (PhD), a member of scientific seminars and qualification exams.

During the master class, Professor R. Karimov highlighted the main milestones of the formation and development of ancient philosophy and culture, its main representatives, and also noted the place that ancient culture occupies in the history of world civilization. Separately, attention was paid to the issues of education and training in Ancient Greece and Ancient Rome, which created the foundation for the development of subsequent European culture. Students took an active part in the master class, asked various questions, and had a lively discussion.

title: Happy New Year - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Happy New Year
content: On these joyful days, I heartily congratulate you on the New Year. I wish you family well-being and good health! Of course, every year that has passed allows us to feel the value of time, summarize everything that we have done this year, outline plans and set goals for the coming year. In 2022, we have achieved many achievements and great successes. And there is undoubtedly a great contribution of each member of our team in this. I congratulate all of you on the coming year 2023, I wish you family happiness and prosperity. May there be an abundance of joy in the house of each of us in the new year. May peace and tranquility reign in our homeland.

title: The winners of the “TIUE CHESS CHAMPIONSHIP 2022” tournament have been determined!!! - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: The winners of the “TIUE CHESS CHAMPIONSHIP 2022” tournament have been determined!!!
content:
A chess tournament “TIUE CHESS CHAMPIONSHIP 2022” was held among the students of the Tashkent International University of Education and the winners were determined.

On behalf of the University administration, we once again congratulate all participants of the tournament on their victory!!!

title: TIUE students of the Navoi National Library - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE students of the Navoi National Library
content:
Today in Uzbekistan, the President and the Government of the country pay great attention to the development of reading culture, especially among the younger generation. In order to implement concrete practical measures in this area, a National program for the development and support of reading Culture was adopted in 2020.

On 21 December, students of the Information Technology direction of the Tashkent International University of Education visited the Alisher Navoi National Library of Uzbekistan as part of a field trip – the largest library in the republic, which contains various artistic, scientific and popular publications in Uzbek and foreign languages.

The purpose of the visit was to get acquainted with the information technologies that are implemented in the National Library of Uzbekistan, namely, the consolidated electronic catalog, which collected the bibliographic catalog of more than 2000 libraries of Uzbekistan. The students also got acquainted with the procedure of providing interactive services to readers, including digitization of manuscripts and access to foreign electronic resources. The login and password received by the ID card gives access to the entrance to the consolidated electronic catalog; RFID technology is used, which accelerates the inventory, search and issuance of books in the library, and RFID tags allow you to monitor the timely return of literature.

title: “The Constitution is the source of our happiness” - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: “The Constitution is the source of our happiness”
content:
Today, on 7 December, Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) organized a round table on the topic “The Constitution is the source of our happiness”, dedicated to the 30th anniversary of the adoption of the Constitution of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

The Constitution is of vital importance for every State, since it is the basic law that regulates all spheres of society, is a guarantee of human rights and freedoms. At the same time, the Constitution is a guarantee of the stability of society.

A round table organized in TIUE on the topic “The Constitution is the source of our happiness” was held by representatives of the Shaykhontokhur District Court for Criminal Cases of the city of Tashkent. During the round table, students were given historical information about the process of drafting and adopting the Constitution of the Republic of Uzbekistan, as well as the importance of the basic law in the life of society. The guests also contributed to the education of students in the spirit of devotion to the Motherland.

Students took an active part in the round table, expressed their opinions, asked various questions and received answers to them.

title: A training seminar from the insurance company "PERFECT INSURANCE" was held at TIUE University - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: A training seminar from the insurance company "PERFECT INSURANCE" was held at TIUE University
content:
On 14 December, a master class on the topic “Business Insurance” was organized at the Tashkent International University of Education for students in the areas of “Business Management” and “Information Technology”.

The master class was conducted by the Chief Manager of the “Insurance Claims Review Department” of the Insurance Organization “PERFECT INSURANCE” Joint Stock Company.

The seminar provided information on legislation and types of insurance related to insurance activities. Insurance procedures are described in detail. Business development indicators were highlighted on the table.

The students actively participated in the conversation with F.U. Nazarov. The questions that were asked from their side were answered with a detailed explanation. Students were interested in the following topics:

title: Today is the day of the adoption of the national anthem of the Republic of Uzbekistan! - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: Today is the day of the adoption of the national anthem of the Republic of Uzbekistan!
content:
The text and music of the National Anthem of the Republic of Uzbekistan were approved by the Law of the Republic of Uzbekistan dated December 10, 1992 “On the National Anthem of the Republic of Uzbekistan”.

Article 2 of this Law states: “The National Anthem of the Republic of Uzbekistan is a symbol of the state sovereignty of the Republic of Uzbekistan. Deep respect for the National Anthem of the Republic of Uzbekistan is a patriotic duty of every citizen of the Republic of Uzbekistan.”

The national anthem of the Republic of Uzbekistan is an expression of the independence of our country. The words of the anthem awaken in the hearts of our citizens feelings of dedication, determination, national pride, patriotism.

The national anthem may be performed during national events, political gatherings, sports competitions, official state meetings, receptions of heads of various foreign states, and other cases provided for by law and with the necessary respect for it.

The words to the National Anthem were written by the People’s poet of Uzbekistan Abdulla Aripov, the music was composed by the People’s Artist, composer Mutal Burkhanov.

title: TIUE professors and lecturers publish their monographs - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE professors and lecturers publish their monographs
content:
Professors and teachers of Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) have published their monographs.

In particular, Ruslan Sultanov, head of the Department of Information Technologies at TIUE, became the author of the monograph “Development of a software package for the formation of educational content based on artificial intelligence.”

The monograph is devoted to the development of a software package that uses artificial intelligence methods to form educational content.

The author describes the basic principles and methods used in the development of the complex, and also suggests specific algorithms and machine learning models that are used in the complex. Particular attention is paid to the development of a system for analyzing student data and creating individual educational trajectories based on this analysis.

Associate Professor of the Department of Information Technology at TIUE Nodir Khodzhaev published a monograph on the topic “Dual education in higher education”.

The work is devoted to the implementation of dual education in the system of higher education, its theoretical and practical foundations and foreign experience in this area, and is intended for pedagogical specialists and all those interested in the problems of dual education and higher education.

The author argues that the use of foreign experience in the organization of dual training will allow, with a skillful competency-based approach, to create a national model of practice-oriented education in Uzbekistan in the shortest possible time, without blindly copying known technologies, but to introduce the positive progressive that is currently acceptable under the current system of education in our country.

Another monograph by Nodir Khodjaev on the topic “Wireless sensor networks” has also been published.

The monograph systematizes numerous information, standards and approaches to the technical implementation of the concept of wireless sensor networks and the Internet of Things (Internet of Things, IoT), as well as related infocommunication technologies (RFID radio frequency identification, WSN wireless sensor networks, M2M machine-to-machine communications).

The protocols and technologies for data transmission are considered, numerous examples of the practical implementation of the Internet of things are given. The basics of economic calculation of the economic efficiency of using wireless sensor networks in various fields of human activity are given.

The monograph is intended for bachelors and masters studying in the direction of “Infocommunication technologies and communication systems”. It can also be useful for students studying in the following areas: “Informatics and Computer Engineering”, “Information Systems and Technologies”, “Intelligent Systems in the Humanities”, “Applied Informatics”.

Associate Professor of the Department of Business Management at TIUE Anush Tsakanyan became the author of the monograph “Methods of Teaching Foreign Languages”.

The monograph provides a description of the methods of teaching foreign languages, indicates the role and place in improving the speech competencies of students.

The study reveals the issue of improving the study of foreign languages in Uzbekistan. The author also highlights the main pedagogical concepts in the context of training teachers and teachers of a foreign language: goals, approaches, principles and methods of teaching. The information given in the monograph can be used in the pedagogical activity of university teachers, be useful for students and undergraduates of universities, as well as in the system of advanced training of public education workers.

title: TIUE student takes part in the opening of the Seeds for the Future 2023 project - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: TIUE student takes part in the opening of the Seeds for the Future 2023 project
content:
On 30 August, an opening ceremony of the Seeds for the Future 2023 project from Huawei took place.

The event was attended by the Minister of Digital Technologies Sherzod Shermatov. The rector of Tashkent International University of Education Nosir Khodjaev also took part in the ceremony.

Submission of applications for participation in the project began in June this year for students of 11 universities of Uzbekistan. According to the selection results, 15 students passed the final interviews with the best results.

Among the project participants is a student of Tashkent International University of Education Kamila Nazarova. Together with 14 students from 7 higher educational institutions of Uzbekistan, she will travel to Qatar to study IT together with project participants from 15 countries of the Middle East and Central Asia.

Minister of Digital Technologies Sherzod Shermatov spoke to the project participants to support them before the trip to Doha, and expressed his opinion on the goals and mission of the project for the development of the IT sector in our country and the significance of this project for the whole world.

Seeds for the Future is Huawei’s global corporate social responsibility program. This project, first launched in Thailand in 2008, aims to develop local ICT talent, broaden their knowledge and horizons, deepen knowledge and interest in telecommunications technology among talented youth, and help the region further expand and support interaction and participation in the digital community. The Seeds for the Future project has been implemented in Uzbekistan for 8 years.

title: A TIUE employee published a scientific article in the journal Biomulecules - Tashkent International University of Education
meta_description: A TIUE employee published a scientific article in the journal Biomulecules
content:
An employee of the Tashkent International University of Education (TIUE) became the author of a scientific article on the topic “Disclosure of the transport properties of RONS along nitrooxidized membranes.” The article was published in Biomulecules, which is a Q1 journal in the Scopus database.

The main indicators of the journal: the impact factor is 5.5, the Scopus Cite Score is 8.3.

The article is devoted to the characteristics of the ability of cold atmospheric plasma (CAP) to generate reactive oxygen and nitrogen species (RONS), which is one of the modern scientific directions.

Cold atmospheric plasma (CAP) is a non-equilibrium plasma operating at atmospheric pressure and room temperature. Based on the research results, the article concludes that PLB nitrooxidation, for example, induced by cold atmospheric plasma, has a minimal effect on overcoming free energy barriers by less hydrophilic particles, while for hydrophilic RONS it reduces barriers and thereby enhances their translocation through nitrooxidases.

This study contributes to a better understanding of the translocation abilities of RONS in plasma biomedical applications and highlights the need for further analysis of their role in intracellular signaling pathways.
